changed.from: There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If the condition fails, a Farkas separating hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from that convex hull. Fix a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\). Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible common \(w\), iid arrays, the independent common target \(T\), and selector auxiliary draws imply, for each arm, \[\Pr\!\left(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k_a)}\mid\mathcal T\right)\le\frac{M+1-k_a}{M+1}=\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}\le\varepsilon_a.\] Clipping to \([-K,K]\) and Bonferroni therefore give \[\Pr\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}\ge1-\varepsilon_0-\varepsilon_1\ge1-\alpha,\] without arm independence. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), one has \(k_0=k_1=k\), \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=I_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\mathcal C_w\), so the symmetric rank and coverage formulas follow as a corollary.
changed.to: Adopt \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\) and \(\operatorname{conv}(\varnothing)=\varnothing\). There exists \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) jointly orbit-balancing both arms if and only if \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\in\operatorname{conv}\{A_{\cdot h}:h\in\mathcal H\}\). Equivalently, the stacked LP in \(\texttt{def:common-selector-feasibility-lp}\) is feasible. If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), infeasibility is certified by \(\sum_{h\in\varnothing}w_h=0\ne1\); no selector mixture, implemented kernel, selector-indexed ordinary or weighted procedure, \(D_*\), \(\mathcal W_*\), or \(w_*\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\ne\varnothing\) and the condition fails, a strict Farkas hyperplane separates \(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\) from the nonempty convex hull. Fix a prespecified \(\boldsymbol\varepsilon=(\varepsilon_0,\varepsilon_1)\) with \(\varepsilon_a>0\) and \(\varepsilon_0+\varepsilon_1\le\alpha\). Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), a fixed feasible common \(w\), iid arrays, the independent common target \(T\), and selector auxiliary draws imply, for each arm, \[\Pr\!\left(S_{0,a}>r_{a,(k_a)}\mid\mathcal T\right)\le\frac{M+1-k_a}{M+1}=\rho_{M,\varepsilon_a}\le\varepsilon_a.\] Clipping to \([-K,K]\) and Bonferroni therefore give \[\Pr\!\left(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\right)\ge1-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_0}-\rho_{M,\varepsilon_1}\ge1-\varepsilon_0-\varepsilon_1\ge1-\alpha,\] without arm independence. Under \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), one has \(k_0=k_1=k\), \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{a,w}=I_{a,w}\), and \(\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\mathcal C_w\), so the symmetric rank and coverage formulas follow as a corollary.
changed.reason: The blanket separating-hyperplane clause is false for an empty selector library. The replacement preserves the existence equivalence under explicit empty-set conventions, supplies the correct normalization contradiction when the library is empty, and restricts geometric separation to the nonempty finite convex hull where it is valid.

changed.from: Let \(\epsilon_0,\epsilon_1>0\) be prespecified and satisfy \(\epsilon_0+\epsilon_1\le\alpha\), and let arm \(a\)'s weighted threshold in \(\texttt{def:weighted-rank-inversion}\) use its left-continuous \((1-\epsilon_a)\)-quantile. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\epsilon_0-\epsilon_1\ge1-\alpha\] for every fixed or prespecified \(w\), every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so the orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance, every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), and \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=r_{a,(k_a)}\) for \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\epsilon_a)\rceil\), including the \(+\infty\) clause when \(k_a=M+1\); hence weighted inversion recovers ordinary arm-specific rank inversion. Taking \(\epsilon_0=\epsilon_1=\alpha/2\) gives \(k_0=k_1=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\) and recovers the original symmetric statement and formulas.
changed.to: Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, and let \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) be fixed or prespecified. Let \(\epsilon_0,\epsilon_1>0\) be prespecified and satisfy \(\epsilon_0+\epsilon_1\le\alpha\), and let arm \(a\)'s weighted threshold in \(\texttt{def:weighted-rank-inversion}\) use its left-continuous \((1-\epsilon_a)\)-quantile. Conditional on \(\mathcal T\), orbit overlap implies \[\Pr_P(\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\mathrm W}_w\mid\mathcal T)\ge1-\epsilon_0-\epsilon_1\ge1-\alpha\] for every \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) and arbitrary fixed measurable trained predictor maps. Invariance makes score laws coincide within an orbit, so the orbit likelihood ratios suffice even with member-level selector singularity. Under joint balance, every relevant \(\lambda_{a,O}=1\), and \(r^{\mathrm W}_{a,w}=r_{a,(k_a)}\) for \(k_a=\lceil(M+1)(1-\epsilon_a)\rceil\), including the \(+\infty\) clause when \(k_a=M+1\); hence weighted inversion recovers ordinary arm-specific rank inversion. Taking \(\epsilon_0=\epsilon_1=\alpha/2\) gives \(k_0=k_1=\lceil(M+1)(1-\alpha/2)\rceil\) and recovers the original symmetric statement and formulas. The one-arm rank inequality is the finite-support likelihood-ratio weighted-exchangeability validity argument; the design-specific deductions are the orbit-quotient likelihood-ratio reduction, the sufficiency of quotient overlap under member-level singularity, and its integration with one common target, one common two-arm selector mixture, clipping, and Bonferroni.
changed.reason: A selector mixture and the weighted procedure are instantiated only when the finite library is nonempty. Restricting the theorem to an actual \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\) repairs the empty-library consumer, while the final sentence narrows the theorem-level positioning to the finite orbit-quotient and common-target adaptations rather than claiming generic weighted-conformal novelty.

changed.from: For a pair block \(b\), \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\). Its arm-specific orbits are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).
changed.to: Fix a pair block \(b\) with \(\mathcal V_b=\{v_{b,0},v_{b,1}\}\) and suppose the local swap subgroup satisfies \(\langle s_b\rangle\le\mathcal G\). Then \(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\) for \(r,a\in\{0,1\}\), with \(s_b\) fixing all outside members and relabelling complete records as in \(\texttt{def:group-action}\), and the global \(\mathcal G\)-orbits in block \(b\) are \(O_{b,0}=\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\}\) and \(O_{b,1}=\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\}\). If the target arm-\(1\) lifted law is supported on \(O_{b,1}\), an exclusive mate-control analogue supported on the realized mate's control state in \(O_{b,0}\) has a different orbit-mass vector, cannot satisfy exact joint balance, and is not a legal arm-\(1\) selector. Separately, if a legal implemented arm-\(1\) selector satisfies \(q^{\mathrm{obs}}_{w,1}(O_{b,1})=0\), then arm \(1\) satisfies the disjoint-orbit premise. Its zero-coverage conclusion applies when \(k\le M\); for \(k=M+1\), coverage is one by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\).
changed.reason: Without \(\langle s_b\rangle\le\mathcal G\), the two displayed states need not be in one global orbit. The added local subgroup condition is exactly the missing specialization and does not strengthen \(\mathcal G\) elsewhere.

Thus \\(O_{b,0}=\\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\\}\\) and \\(O_{b,1}=\\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\\}\\).","inputs":["\\(S_2\\)","\\(v_{b,r}\\)","\\(O_{b,a}\\)","\\(D_i\\)"]}

Under that condition, \\(s_b(v_{b,r},a)=(v_{b,1-r},a)\\) for \\(r,a\\in\\{0,1\\}\\), \\(s_b\\) fixes members outside block \\(b\\), and it relabels complete records by \\(\\texttt{def:group-action}\\). Because every group element preserves blocks and block \\(b\\) has exactly two members, the global \\(\\mathcal G\\)-orbits in that block are \\(O_{b,0}=\\{(v_{b,0},0),(v_{b,1},0)\\}\\) and \\(O_{b,1}=\\{(v_{b,0},1),(v_{b,1},1)\\}\\). For a collection of pair blocks, every invoked local subgroup must be contained in \\(\\mathcal G\\); the standard \\(S_2^B\\) specialization contains the product of all relevant swaps.","inputs":["\\(\\mathcal G\\)","\\(\\mathcal V_b\\)","\\(S_2\\)","\\(v_{b,r}\\)","\\(O_{b,a}\\)","\\(D_i\\)"]}

changed.from:
{"id":"def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector","name":"minimum-imbalance common selector","construction":"Assume that \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty. Define
\[
D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\{\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,
\]
and
\[
\mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\{\Delta_0(w)+\Delta_1(w)\}.
\]
An explicit finite absolute-value linear program for \(D_*\) is
\[
\begin{aligned}
\operatorname*{minimize}_{w,(t_{a,O})}\quad &\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}t_{a,O}\\
\text{subject to}\quad &-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O}&&\forall a\in\{0,1\},\ O\in\mathcal O,\\
&t_{a,O}\ge0&&\forall a\in\{0,1\},\ O\in\mathcal O,\\
&w_h\ge0&&\forall h\in\mathcal H,\\
&\sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1.
\end{aligned}
\]
Its associated quantitative dual program is
\[
\operatorname*{maximize}_{y\in\mathbb R^{2|\mathcal O|}:\,\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
The equality and attainment of the primal and dual values are certified by \(\texttt{thm:minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).","inputs":["\(\mathcal H\)","\(A\)","\(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\)","\(\Delta_a(w)\)","\(D_*\)","\(\mathcal W_*\)","\(w\)"]}
changed.to: {"id":"def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector","name":"minimum-imbalance common selector","construction":"For every finite selector library define \\(\\Delta(\\mathcal H)=\\{w\\in\\mathbb R^{|\\mathcal H|}:w_h\\ge0\\ \\forall h,\\ \\sum_{h\\in\\mathcal H}w_h=1\\}\\); in particular \\(\\Delta(\\varnothing)=\\varnothing\\), because the empty sum is zero. If \\(\\mathcal H=\\varnothing\\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, weighted procedure, minimum \\(D_*\\), optimizer set \\(\\mathcal W_*\\), or choice \\(w_*\\) exists. If \\(\\mathcal H\\) is finite and nonempty, define \\[D_*=\\min_{w\\in\\Delta(\\mathcal H)}\\{\\Delta_0(w)+\\Delta_1(w)\\}=\\min_{w\\in\\Delta(\\mathcal H)}\\frac12\\sum_{a=0}^1\\sum_{O\\in\\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\\qquad \\mathcal W_*=\\operatorname*{arg\\,min}_{w\\in\\Delta(\\mathcal H)}\\{\\Delta_0(w)+\\Delta_1(w)\\}.\\] The finite absolute-value linear program is \\[\\begin{aligned}\\operatorname*{minimize}_{w,(t_{a,O})}\\quad&\\frac12\\sum_{a=0}^1\\sum_{O\\in\\mathcal O}t_{a,O}\\\\\\text{subject to}\\quad&-t_{a,O}\\le(Aw-b_{\\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\\le t_{a,O},\\quad t_{a,O}\\ge0,\\\\&w_h\\ge0,\\quad\\sum_{h\\in\\mathcal H}w_h=1.\\end{aligned}\\] Its quantitative dual is \\[\\operatorname*{maximize}_{\\lVert y\\rVert_\\infty\\le1/2}\\left\\{\\min_{h\\in\\mathcal H}y^\\top A_{\\cdot h}-y^\\top b_{\\mathrm{LP}}\\right\\}.\\] These primal and dual objects are defined only in the finite nonempty case; their equality and attainment are certified by \\(\\texttt{thm:minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\\).","inputs":["\\(\\mathcal H\\)","\\(A\\)","\\(b_{\\mathrm{LP}}\\)","\\(\\Delta_a(w)\\)","\\(D_*\\)","\\(\\mathcal W_*\\)","\\(w\\)"]}
changed.reason: This makes the empty-library normalization contradiction explicit and prevents undefined minima, kernels, and weighted procedures from being asserted when no selector exists.

note: Replace false pair-subgroup necessity by a conservative sufficient audit rule
directive: Preserve the accepted S_2 symbol correction and all prior D0.5 root repairs. Replace the rejected necessity-bearing O_{b,a} and def:pair-action edits with the exact conservative sufficient rule: O_{b,a}={(v_{b,0},a),(v_{b,1},a)} denotes a two-state pair cell unconditionally. If V_b={v_{b,0},v_{b,1}} and the pure local swap subgroup <s_b> is contained in G, then O_{b,a} is a global G-orbit. Absent that audit, the notation O_{b,a} alone makes no global-orbit claim. Do NOT say “only if,” “if and only if,” or otherwise claim the pure local subgroup condition is necessary, because coupled-swap subgroups may also generate the same orbit. Retain the conservative invocation policy: pair-specific propositions and examples may be invoked only after this sufficient local audit (and product examples verify each required local swap). Synchronize the O_{b,a} symbol, def:pair-action, prop:s2-reduction, all pair/frontier consumers and proofs, TLDR/setup/scope prose, and reverse dependencies. Do not alter G globally, the one common target, empty-library handling, weighted validity, verified citations, or any evaluator theorem/resource bound.

note: Maximality improvement: active finite-rank arm imbalance and active-row selector LP
directive:
Implement the strict boundary-regime sharpening under the same assumptions, preserving every current theorem not explicitly changed. For the prespecified armwise ranks define A_epsilon={a in {0,1}: k_a<=M}. In prop:asymmetric-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound strengthen the fixed-w guarantee to
P{tau_T in C_w^epsilon | Tcal} >= max{0, 1 - sum_{a in A_epsilon}(rho_{M,epsilon_a}+Delta_a(w))}.
Justify omission of each boundary arm using prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary: when k_a=M+1, I_{a,w}^epsilon=[-K,K] contains Y_{0T}(a) surely after clipping, so its orbit imbalance cannot contribute to effect-set miscoverage.

For finite nonempty H, extend def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector with the active-row objective
D_{*,A}=min_{w in Delta(H)} (1/2) sum_{a in A_epsilon, O in O}|(Aw-b_LP)_{a,O}|,
W_{*,A}=argmin of that objective,
using the same absolute-value LP restricted to active-arm rows. Give the exact dual
max_{||y||_infinity<=1/2, y_{a,O}=0 for a notin A_epsilon}{min_h y^T A_{.h} - y^T b_LP}.
Prespecify any w_{*,A} in W_{*,A} and strengthen thm:minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate to
P{tau_T in C_{w_{*,A}}^epsilon | Tcal} >= max{0,1-sum_{a in A_epsilon}rho_{M,epsilon_a}-D_{*,A}}.
If A_epsilon is empty and H is finite nonempty, define D_{*,A}=0 and W_{*,A}=Delta(H), return coverage one without solving an LP. Retain the rule that no selector-indexed procedure, w, D_*, D_{*,A}, W_*, W_{*,A}, w_*, or w_{*,A} exists when H is empty. Keep the existing D_* and W_* unchanged as joint two-arm balance diagnostics: D_{*,A}=0 characterizes balance only on active arms and must not be advertised as common two-arm balance.

Synchronize new symbols, definitions, assumptions, statements, dependencies, proofs, consumers, TLDR, project justification, interpretation, honest scope, technical limitations, and theorem notes. Explicitly highlight the one-boundary elbow and the two-boundary trivial case. Leave the one common target, quotient-weighted conformal results, pair audit, shared-assignment R_w, coherent P_T, exact frontier, global memo, all three evaluator branches, and branch-conditional resource accounting unchanged.

changed.to:
{"id":"def:minimum-imbalance-common-selector","name":"minimum-imbalance common selector","construction":"For every finite selector library define \(\Delta(\mathcal H)=\{w\in\mathbb R^{|\mathcal H|}:w_h\ge0\ \forall h,\ \sum_{h\in\mathcal H}w_h=1\}\); in particular \(\Delta(\varnothing)=\varnothing\). If \(\mathcal H=\varnothing\), no selector mixture, implemented kernel, selector-indexed ordinary or weighted procedure, \(D_*\), \(D_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(\mathcal W_*\), \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\), \(w_*\), or \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) exists. If \(\mathcal H\) is finite and nonempty, retain the joint two-arm diagnostics
\[
D_*=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_*=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
Their absolute-value linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a=0}^1\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to \(-t_{a,O}\le(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}\le t_{a,O}\), \(t_{a,O}\ge0\), \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and their dual is
\[
\max_{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
For the prespecified armwise ranks define \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\{a\in\{0,1\}:k_a\le M\}\) and
\[
D_{*,\mathcal A}=\min_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|,\qquad
\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\operatorname*{arg\,min}_{w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)}\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_{O\in\mathcal O}|(Aw-b_{\mathrm{LP}})_{a,O}|.
\]
The active-row linear program minimizes \(\frac12\sum_{a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}\sum_Ot_{a,O}\) subject to the same two-sided constraints only for \(a\in\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}\), together with \(t_{a,O}\ge0\) and \(w\in\Delta(\mathcal H)\). Its dual is
\[
\max_{\substack{\lVert y\rVert_\infty\le1/2\\y_{a,O}=0\ \mathrm{for}\ a\notin\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}}}\left\{\min_{h\in\mathcal H}y^\top A_{\cdot h}-y^\top b_{\mathrm{LP}}\right\}.
\]
If \(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}=\varnothing\), define \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) and \(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}=\Delta(\mathcal H)\), and choose \(w_{*,\mathcal A}\) without solving an LP. The full objects \(D_*\) and \(\mathcal W_*\) remain the joint two-arm balance diagnostics; \(D_{*,\mathcal A}=0\) characterizes only active-row balance unless both arms are active. Primal and dual equality, attainment, and the coverage certificates are proved in \(\texttt{thm:active-arm-minimum-imbalance-selector-certificate}\).","inputs":["\(\mathcal H\)","\(A\)","\(b_{\mathrm{LP}}\)","\(\Delta_a(w)\)","\(\boldsymbol\varepsilon\)","\(k_a\)","\(\mathcal A_{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}\)","\(D_*\)","\(D_{*,\mathcal A}\)","\(\mathcal W_*\)","\(\mathcal W_{*,\mathcal A}\)","\(w_*\)","\(w_{*,\mathcal A}\)","\(w\)"]}

note: maximality-18 formal hybrid evaluator resource statement
directive:
Promote the already-proved three-way exact evaluator from explanatory proof text and consumer guidance into the formal resource statements of exactly these three targets: lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator, thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier, and thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier. Define a global exact-key partition Pi_* = Pi_roll disjoint-union Pi_MS disjoint-union Pi_+ according to the selected exact branch for each distinct memoized key. Replace the coarse terminal GM k_0 k_1 (and symmetric GM k^2) charge by the branch-adaptive sum
  sum_{pi in Pi_roll} C_{M,k_0,k_1}
  + sum_{pi in Pi_MS} (M + T_{M,bar k_0,bar k_1})
  + sum_{pi in Pi_+} C_+(pi),
plus exactly one shared O(M) factorial-preprocessing term iff Pi_MS is nonempty. State branch-conditional recurrence workspace as the maximum over branches actually selected: O(bar k_0 bar k_1) for rolling, O(M) for multinomial-sum, and O(min{t_0^sigma,t_1^sigma}) for the oriented positive-corner coefficient branch, while retaining the G memoized scalar answers and every existing non-recurrence table term. On the symmetric diagonal define bar k=min{k,M-k+1}. Make the empty-branch maxima/sums explicit and mathematically well-defined. Synchronize the writeup theorem statements, complexity discussion, abstract/introduction/conclusion headline claims, and any tables or consumer guidance so no coarse bound is presented as the final formal headline. Preserve every exact key, local coherent weight, P_T^C, R_w, common selector w, common target, all probabilistic claims, and all existing active-arm, boundary, pair-audit, empty-library, and weighted-conformal semantics. The required strict witness is k_0=k_1=M, for which bar k_0=bar k_1=1 and the rolling per-key charge improves from Theta(M^3) to Theta(M), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.

changed.from:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\) and the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\).

A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and runs the recurrence only \(G\) times. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right)
\]
unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including the current local exact-key table and the global memo, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\).
changed.to:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Break ties prespecifically, and let
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\]
be the resulting exact-key partition. For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) be a minimizing positive-base orientation in \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), with oriented thresholds \(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where every empty sum is zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
Thus empty-branch maxima are harmless. A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and evaluates only the \(G\) distinct keys. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing charge and is absent when \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}=\varnothing\). If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including all non-recurrence tables, the current local exact-key table, and the \(G\) memoized scalar answers, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). In particular, if \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), so the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.
changed.reason: The current resource conclusion is a valid but coarse envelope. The directive requires the formal claim to be restricted to the explicitly selected three-branch exact evaluator and to report its strictly sharper branch-adaptive charge and actual-branch workspace.

changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), and \(R_w\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). For preorder pair \(C\), let \(P_T^C\) be the coherent target pushforward, let \(L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|\), let \(\Pi_C\) be the set of distinct supported exact prefix-table vectors \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\), and put \(U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). Retain the local coherent weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) obtained by aggregating \(P_T^C\) mass over identical vectors. Let \(\mathfrak C\) contain all \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
The global recurrence memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each recurrence value \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) is computed once globally, while the differing weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global balanced-tree lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+GMk_0k_1
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+GMk_0k_1\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k_0k_1+U_{\max}+G)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence global memoization preserves both calibration-arm dependence and the one common target.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan.

If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. \[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]

With prespecified tie-breaking, write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]

\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).

Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).

The leading \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence memoization and branch selection preserve calibration-arm dependence, the one common target, and the common selector. If \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and the rolling per-key charge is \(\Theta(M)\), rather than the coarse \(\Theta(M^3)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.

whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey

At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses

arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. \[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.

Thus \(P_T\) preserves the one common target and \(R_w\) preserves the selectors' shared-assignment dependence. \[

\]
If \(k\le M\), a global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each coupled recurrence value once. unit-cost exact comparisons and arithmetic operations. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[

\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+k^2+U_{\max}+G)\). changed.to:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders; every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Retain the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\), local weights \(\omega_C\), exact vectors \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G=|\Pi_\star|\) from \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). If \(k\le M\), put
\[
\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
For every exact key \(\pi\), deterministically select a least-charge evaluator among the rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\), the multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\), and the positive-corner charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), and write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k^2:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
A global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each exact probability once, while differing local weights are applied only to their own preorder objectives. Exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), the uniform bound replaces the two middle sums by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]
\]
and retains the terminal \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\). Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. Every exact key is constructed from unchanged \(R_w\), and every local weight from unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the common target, common selector, and cross-arm dependence are preserved. At \(k=M\), \(\bar k=1\) and the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).

Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi=(\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10},\pi_{11})\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) independent draws with this law, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) and \(\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\) are the keys assigned to the two branches, the exact aggregate resource charge is
\[
O\!\left(M+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right)
\]
operations, with the \(O(M)\) factorial preprocessing shared once, four probability-specific power tables rebuilt for each \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\), sequential key processing, and \(O(|\Pi|+\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\) memory when one scalar answer per key is retained.

\[
\begin{aligned}

={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then both counts are deterministic and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\},
\]
which is evaluable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Partition only the nonvertex keys between the two generic branches,
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}.
\]
The exact aggregate resource charge is
\[
O\!\left(|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right)
\]
operations. The \(O(M)\) factorial preprocessing is shared once if and only if the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty; four probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\). Sequential processing and retention of one scalar answer per key use \(O(|\Pi|+\max\{1,M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\})\) memory. The vertex prepass precedes the corner-complemented three-way dispatcher in downstream use, so vertex keys enter neither a rolling, multinomial-sum, nor positive-corner coefficient branch.
changed.reason: The generic-dispatch clause is narrowed to nonvertex keys after a deterministic simplex-vertex stratum is resolved exactly; the count formula itself is unchanged.

changed.from: Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). For \((x,y)\in\{0,1\}^2\), define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). Let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\). If both ranks are at most \(M\), then the recurrence \(d_0(0,0)=1\) and \(d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)\), with out-of-range entries zero and only \(0\le r<k_0\), \(0\le s<k_1\) retained, satisfies \[\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)=\Pr\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\quad \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\}.\] After \(R\) is tabulated, the four cells and recurrence require \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), the corresponding event is certain and a one-dimensional recurrence for the other count requires \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory, where \(k_c\le M\) is the other rank. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint event has probability one and requires no recurrence.
changed.to:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). For \((x,y)\in\{0,1\}^2\), define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). Let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\). If both ranks are at most \(M\), then the recurrence \(d_0(0,0)=1\) and \(d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)\), with out-of-range entries zero and only \(0\le r<k_0\), \(0\le s<k_1\) retained, satisfies \[\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)=\Pr\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\quad \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\}.\] After \(R\) is tabulated, the four cells and recurrence require \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), the corresponding event is certain and a one-dimensional recurrence for the other count requires \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory, where \(k_c\le M\) is the other rank. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint event has probability one and requires no recurrence. For any finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then the two terminal counts are \(Mx\) and \(My\) surely, so
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\]
Thus the simplex-vertex prepass evaluates each such key in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace before any recurrence; only nonvertex two-finite-rank keys require a generic exact evaluator.
changed.reason: The recurrence claim is restricted to keys that reach it, while simplex vertices receive the exact deterministic-count formula required by the directive.

changed.from:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Break ties prespecifically, and let
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\]
be the resulting exact-key partition. For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) be a minimizing positive-base orientation in \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), with oriented thresholds \(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where every empty sum is zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
Thus empty-branch maxima are harmless. A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and evaluates only the \(G\) distinct keys. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing charge and is absent when \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}=\varnothing\). If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including all non-recurrence tables, the current local exact-key table, and the \(G\) memoized scalar answers, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). In particular, if \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), so the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.

Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
Define the simplex-vertex stratum
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), the exact value is \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\), computable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace.
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Break ties prespecifically, and let
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\]
be the resulting exact-key partition. For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) be a minimizing positive-base orientation in \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), with oriented thresholds \(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where every empty sum is zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
Thus empty-branch maxima are harmless. A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and evaluates only the \(G\) distinct keys. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing charge and is absent when \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}=\varnothing\). If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including all non-recurrence tables, the current local exact-key table, and the \(G\) memoized scalar answers, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). In particular, if \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), so the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).
changed.reason: The dispatcher is narrowed to the nonvertex stratum and the total charge is sharpened by an exact constant-time vertex prepass without changing any probability object.

changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), \(R_w\), \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), \(\omega_C\), \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Thus \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), the global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), and \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each exact four-cell probability is computed once globally, while the differing local coherent weights are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among the corner-oriented rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\), the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\), and the positive-corner coefficient charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). With prespecified tie-breaking, write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The leading \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence memoization and branch selection preserve calibration-arm dependence, the one common target, and the common selector. If \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and the rolling per-key charge is \(\Theta(M)\), rather than the coarse \(\Theta(M^3)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), \(R_w\), \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), \(\omega_C\), \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\) in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Thus \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), the global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), and \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each exact four-cell probability is computed once globally, while the differing local coherent weights are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among the corner-oriented rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\), the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\), and the positive-corner coefficient charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). With prespecified tie-breaking, write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The leading \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence memoization and branch selection preserve calibration-arm dependence, the one common target, and the common selector. If \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and the rolling per-key charge is \(\Theta(M)\), rather than the coarse \(\Theta(M^3)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).

changed.from:
{"id":"lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","kind":"lemma","statement":"Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi=(\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10},\pi_{11})\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) independent draws with this law, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then both counts are deterministic and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\},
\]
which is evaluable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Partition only the nonvertex keys between the two generic branches,
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}.
\]
The exact aggregate resource charge is
\[
O\!\left(|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right)
\]
operations. The \(O(M)\) factorial preprocessing is shared once if and only if the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty; four probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\). Sequential processing and retention of one scalar answer per key use \(O(|\Pi|+\max\{1,M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\})\) memory. The vertex prepass precedes the corner-complemented three-way dispatcher in downstream use, so vertex keys enter neither a rolling, multinomial-sum, nor positive-corner coefficient branch.","depends_on":["lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator"],"status":"proved","justification":"The feasible count-vector bijection gives a zero-cell-safe exact evaluator and a reference branch against which the coefficient and rolling recurrences are compared.","gap":"Probability-specific power tables must be rebuilt per multinomial key even though factorial preprocessing is shared.","consumer":"Process exact keys sequentially, share factorials, and use \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\) only as one branch of the three-way time comparison.","proof_tex":"Let \(N_{xy}\) be the number of the \(M\) draws equal to \((x,y)\), and put \(R=N_{10}+N_{11}\) and \(S=N_{01}+N_{11}\). If \(R=r\), \(S=s\), and \(N_{11}=t\), then necessarily
\[
(N_{00},N_{01},N_{10},N_{11})=(M-r-s+t,s-t,r-t,t).
\tag{1}
\]
Conversely, (1) is a nonnegative integer vector of total \(M\) with margins \((r,s)\) exactly when
\[
\max\{0,r+s-M\}\le t\le\min\{r,s\}.
\tag{2}
\]
The multinomial probability of (1) is
\[
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\tag{3}
\]
Summing (3) over (2), and then over \(0\le r<k_0\) and \(0\le s<k_1\), proves the displayed formula. The conventions \(0^0=1\) and \(0^j=0\) for \(j>0\) make (3) valid on every face of the simplex. For each \((r,s)\), the number of feasible \(t\)'s is at most \(\min\{r,s\}+1\le\min\{k_0,k_1\}\), whence
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}.
\tag{4}
\]
Precomputing the factorials through \(M!\) takes \(O(M)\) arithmetic operations. For a fixed key, repeated multiplication constructs the four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and \(O(M)\) memory, after which each feasible summand in (3) costs constant time. This proves the multinomial charge. The elementary two-count convolution, retaining only \(r<k_0\) and \(s<k_1\), is exact by induction on the number of draws and uses \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) rolling memory. Thus the per-key minimum and branch-conditional memory bounds follow.

It remains to justify the prepass and aggregate charge. Let \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\). Since the four nonnegative cells sum to one, the cell \((x,y)\) satisfying \(\pi_{xy}=1\) is unique and every other cell is zero. Every draw therefore equals \((x,y)\) almost surely, so
\[
(R,S)=(Mx,My)\quad\text{almost surely},
\qquad
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\tag{5}
\]
Checking four cells and the two inequalities in (5) costs \(O(1)\) time and workspace and uses neither factorials nor power tables. Apply this test before partitioning the remaining keys into \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) and \(\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\). Sequentially summing their certified charges gives
\[
O\!\left(|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right).
\tag{6}
\]
The factorial table in (6) is built if and only if the nonvertex multinomial branch is used. Only one branch workspace is live at a time, and retaining one answer per key adds \(O(|\Pi|)\) storage, proving the proposed memory statement. Coordinate complementation only permutes the four cells, so it preserves vertex status; consequently the same prepass is valid before the downstream corner-oriented three-way dispatcher."}
