{"id":"lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","kind":"lemma","statement":"Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi=(\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10},\pi_{11})\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) independent draws with this law, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then both counts are deterministic and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\},
\]
which is evaluable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Partition only the nonvertex keys between the two generic branches,
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}.
\]
The exact aggregate resource charge is
\[
O\!\left(|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right)
\]
operations. The \(O(M)\) factorial preprocessing is shared once if and only if the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty; four probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\). Sequential processing and retention of one scalar answer per key use \(O(|\Pi|+\max\{1,M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\})\) memory. The vertex prepass precedes the corner-complemented three-way dispatcher in downstream use, so vertex keys enter neither a rolling, multinomial-sum, nor positive-corner coefficient branch.","depends_on":["lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass"],"status":"proved","justification":"The feasible count-vector bijection gives a zero-cell-safe exact evaluator and a reference branch against which the coefficient and rolling recurrences are compared.","gap":"Probability-specific power tables must be rebuilt per multinomial key even though factorial preprocessing is shared.","consumer":"Process exact keys sequentially, share factorials, and use \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\) only as one branch of the three-way time comparison.","proof_tex":"Let \(N_{xy}\) be the number of the \(M\) draws equal to \((x,y)\), and put \(R=N_{10}+N_{11}\) and \(S=N_{01}+N_{11}\). If \(R=r\), \(S=s\), and \(N_{11}=t\), then necessarily
\[

\tag{1}
\]
Conversely, (1) is a nonnegative integer vector of total \(M\) with margins \((r,s)\) exactly when
\[

\]
The multinomial probability of (1) is

\]
Summing (3) over (2), and then over \(0\le r<k_0\) and \(0\le s<k_1\), proves the displayed formula. The conventions \(0^0=1\) and \(0^j=0\) for \(j>0\) make (3) valid on every face of the simplex. For each \((r,s)\), the number of feasible \(t\)'s is at most \(\min\{r,s\}+1\le\min\{k_0,k_1\}\), whence

The elementary two-count convolution, retaining only \(r<k_0\) and \(s<k_1\), is exact by induction on the number of draws and uses \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) rolling memory. Thus the per-key minimum and branch-conditional memory bounds follow.

\tag{6}
\]

{"id":"lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","kind":"lemma","statement":"Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). For \((x,y)\in\{0,1\}^2\), define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). Let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\). If both ranks are at most \(M\), then the recurrence \(d_0(0,0)=1\) and \(d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)\), with out-of-range entries zero and only \(0\le r<k_0\), \(0\le s<k_1\) retained, satisfies \[\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)=\Pr\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\quad \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\}.\] After \(R\) is tabulated, the four cells and recurrence require \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), the corresponding event is certain and a one-dimensional recurrence for the other count requires \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory, where \(k_c\le M\) is the other rank. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint event has probability one and requires no recurrence. For any finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then the two terminal counts are \(Mx\) and \(My\) surely, so
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\]
Thus the simplex-vertex prepass evaluates each such key in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace before any recurrence; only nonvertex two-finite-rank keys require a generic exact evaluator.","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator"],"status":"proved","justification":"The retained-state induction proves exactness on every simplex face and supplies the rolling branch of the three-way evaluator.","gap":"The coarse \(Mk_0k_1\) charge hides both corner complementation and positive-corner coefficient savings.","consumer":"Use the least exact operation count among the rolling corner, multinomial sum, and positive-corner coefficient branches, but charge the workspace of that same selected branch.","proof_tex":"First consider the simplex-vertex prepass. If \(\pi_{xy}=1\), nonnegativity and \(\sum_{x,y}\pi_{xy}=1\) imply that this unit cell is unique. Every iid increment equals \((x,y)\) almost surely. Hence the terminal strict-below counts are exactly \((Mx,My)\), and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\tag{1}
\]
Testing the four cells and the two inequalities in (1) takes constant exact time and constant workspace, including when a rank equals \(M+1\). Thus a vertex key is removed before any recurrence.

For a general key and \(0\le i\le M\), put
\[
R_i=\sum_{j=1}^i b_0(E_{j,0}),\qquad S_i=\sum_{j=1}^i b_1(E_{j,1}).
\]
We prove by induction that each retained entry satisfies \(d_i(r,s)=\Pr\{R_i=r,S_i=s\}\). This is the initialization at \(i=0\). Independence of draw \(i+1\) and the definition of the four cells give
\[
\Pr\{R_{i+1}=r,S_{i+1}=s\}
=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}\Pr\{R_i=r-x,S_i=s-y\}.
\tag{2}
\]
Negative indices have probability zero. Because both increments are nonnegative, a discarded state cannot return to the retained rectangle. Equation (2) is therefore exactly the asserted recurrence, and summing its terminal entries over \(r<k_0\), \(s<k_1\) proves the probability identity.

One scan of the table of \(R\) forms the four cells in \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|)\) operations. Each of the \(M\) updates visits at most \(k_0k_1\) states, and two rolling arrays use \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If, say, \(k_0=M+1\), then \(R_i<k_0\) is certain. Marginalizing the four cells over the first coordinate yields the analogous one-dimensional induction for \(S_i\), with \(O(Mk_1)\) operations and \(O(k_1)\) memory after tabulation. The other one-boundary case is symmetric, and with both ranks equal to \(M+1\) the joint event is certain. These arguments prove all proposed exactness and resource claims. The corner-oriented, multinomial-sum, and positive-corner refinements in the declared dependencies apply only after the constant-time vertex test and do not alter (2)."}

{"id":"lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","kind":"lemma","statement":"Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
Define the simplex-vertex stratum
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), the exact value is \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\), computable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace.
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Break ties prespecifically, and let
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\]
be the resulting exact-key partition. For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) be a minimizing positive-base orientation in \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), with oriented thresholds \(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where every empty sum is zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
Thus empty-branch maxima are harmless. A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and evaluates only the \(G\) distinct keys. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing charge and is absent when \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}=\varnothing\). If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including all non-recurrence tables, the current local exact-key table, and the \(G\) memoized scalar answers, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). In particular, if \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), so the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:rank-inversion","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","lem:positive-corner-coefficient-recurrence"],"status":"proved","justification":"Exact prefix vectors permit global reuse of four-cell probabilities while each preorder retains its own coherent target weights and the full joint calibration law remains unchanged.","gap":"Memoizing target weights, factorizing \(R_w\), or minimizing time and memory independently would change the probability object or produce an invalid resource certificate.","consumer":"Memoize only \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), retain each preorder's \(\omega_C\), assign each exact key to the least-charge certified evaluator, and charge workspace to the branches actually selected.","proof_tex":"Aggregate the implemented selector by
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z).
\tag{1}
\]
Conditional on \(Z_i=z\), selector auxiliary randomization makes the two armwise selections independent with kernels (1). Integrating over the known assignment law gives
\[
R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\tag{2}
\]
and these orbit pairs are iid across calibration replicates. The common prespecified target has joint orbit law
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)
\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0, O_1(v)=O_1\}.
\tag{3}
\]
The support of (3) is the image of \(\mathcal V\), so \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). Pushing (3) through a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) cannot increase its support and has range of size \(d_0d_1\), proving
\[
L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}.
\tag{4}
\]

Aggregate (2) over ordered class pairs and form its two-dimensional strict-prefix table. For a target class pair \((j,\ell)\), four prefix combinations give the probabilities that neither, only arm zero, only arm one, or both calibration scores are strictly below the corresponding target scores. This is precisely \(\pi^C(j,\ell)\), and each vector is obtained in constant time. The strict prefix, rather than a weak prefix, handles ties. Conditional on \((j,\ell)\), the two order-statistic events hold exactly when their strict-below counts are below \(k_0\) and \(k_1\). Therefore
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events under }C\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi),
\qquad U_C\le L_C.
\tag{5}
\]
The value \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) depends only on \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), since the fourth cell is determined by summing to one. Thus a global memo evaluates each of the \(G\) exact keys once, while the local weights in (5) remain attached to their own preorder. Equations (2)--(5) prove that neither the shared-assignment dependence nor the common target is factorized or replaced.

We now establish the vertex prepass. If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), its four nonnegative cells sum to one and one cell \((x,y)\) equals one. That cell is unique, every calibration indicator pair equals \((x,y)\), and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\tag{6}
\]
Formula (6) costs \(O(1)\) exact operations and workspace. For every remaining key, the corner-oriented rolling lemma supplies charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\), the zero-cell-safe multinomial lemma supplies charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\), and the positive-corner coefficient lemma supplies charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Each computes the same probability. Deterministic least-charge selection on \(\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) therefore yields
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\tag{7}
\]
and exactly the proposed charge
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\tag{8}
\]
The factorial table in (8) is built once if and only if the nonvertex multinomial set is nonempty. Sequential processing uses the workspace of the selected nonvertex branch; the vertex branch contributes only constant space, absorbed by the constant term in \(\mathsf W\).

The target scan costs \(O(|\mathcal V|)\). For every preorder pair, forming class tables, the strict-prefix table, and the target pushforward costs \(O(m_0m_1+L)\). A local balanced tree costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic; forming (5) costs \(O(U_C)\), and the global memo costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) comparisons. Adding (8) proves the instance-adaptive time display. Replacing \(L_C,U_C\) by \(L_{\max},U_{\max}\) proves the uniform bound. The orbit table, target table, local key table, \(G\) memoized answers, and live evaluator workspace prove
\[
O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)
\]
memory.

Finally consider the strict witness. Take one matched pair, the group \(S_2\), fair one-treated--one-control assignment, and the selector that chooses the unique observed member in each arm. There is one orbit per arm, so both \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and every strict-below indicator is zero; hence \(\pi_{00}=1\). For \(M\ge2\), \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) give \(k_0=k_1=M\). Formula (6) gives \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The old rolling charge is \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), and the multinomial charge is \(M+\mathsf T_{M,1,1}=M+1\). Only the lower--lower orientation has positive base mass, so \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)=\Theta(M^2)\). Hence the prior least-charge dispatcher is \(\Theta(M)\), while (6) is \(\Theta(1)\)."}

{"id":"lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","kind":"lemma","statement":"Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]

\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
Define the simplex-vertex stratum
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), the exact value is \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\), computable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace.
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). \end{aligned}
\]

\[

The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:rank-inversion","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","lem:positive-corner-coefficient-recurrence","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass"],"status":"proved","justification":"Exact prefix vectors permit global reuse of four-cell probabilities while each preorder retains its own coherent target weights and the full joint calibration law remains unchanged.","gap":"Memoizing target weights, factorizing \(R_w\), or minimizing time and memory independently would change the probability object or produce an invalid resource certificate.","consumer":"Memoize only \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), retain each preorder's \(\omega_C\), assign each exact key to the least-charge certified evaluator, and charge workspace to the branches actually selected.","proof_tex":"Aggregate the implemented selector by

{"id":"thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), \(R_w\), \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), \(\omega_C\), \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\) in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Thus \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), the global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), and \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each exact four-cell probability is computed once globally, while the differing local coherent weights are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among the corner-oriented rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\), the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\), and the positive-corner coefficient charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). With prespecified tie-breaking, write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The leading \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence memoization and branch selection preserve calibration-arm dependence, the one common target, and the common selector. If \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and the rolling per-key charge is \(\Theta(M)\), rather than the coarse \(\Theta(M^3)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary","prop:asymmetric-disjoint-orbit-witness","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-equivariance","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator","lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator"],"status":"proved","justification":"The rank event depends only on finite total preorders, allowing exact evaluation in every boundary pattern while preserving one common target and the calibration arms' joint assignment law.","gap":"Binary scores are not universally extremal, and using one two-arm recurrence when a rank equals \(M+1\) wastes an entire preorder enumeration and cross-arm table scan.","consumer":"Use the three-branch exact-key memo when both ranks are finite, the one-arm binomial scan when exactly one rank is infinite, and return zero when both ranks are infinite.","proof_tex":"For a finite rank \(k_a\), the elementary order-statistic identity is
\[
s_a(O_{T,a})\le Q_{k_a}(s_a(O_{1,a}),\ldots,s_a(O_{M,a}))
\iff
\sum_{i=1}^M\mathbf 1\{s_a(O_{i,a})<s_a(O_{T,a})\}<k_a.
\tag{1}
\]
Thus ties are governed by strict prefixes and the event depends on \(s_a\) only through its total preorder. Conversely, if a preorder has ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), assign score zero when \(d_a=1\), and otherwise assign \((j-1)K/(d_a-1)\) to class \(j\). This realizes it in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. The finite set of \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs therefore attains the maximum in the definition, proving exactness over the declared orbit-constant score class and making no assertion about a supremum over latent laws.

Suppose first that both ranks are finite. By \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\), for every preorder pair \(C\),
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events}\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi),
\qquad U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}.
\tag{2}
\]
The exact memo key contains no target weight, so every \(\omega_C\) remains local. If \(\pi_{xy}=1\), all \(M\) strict-prefix indicator pairs equal \((x,y)\), and hence
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\tag{3}
\]
This proves the constant-time, constant-workspace vertex prepass. The coherent evaluator then partitions only the nonvertex keys among the three exact branches, yielding
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\tag{4}
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\tag{5}
\]
The vertex workspace is constant, and the factorial preprocessing in (5) occurs exactly when the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty. Substitution of (5) into the evaluator's grouping and memo accounting proves both displayed time bounds and the stated \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\) memory bound. Since the construction of each key uses the unchanged full \(R_w\), and its local weight uses the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the prepass and dispatcher preserve calibration-arm dependence, the common target, and the common selector.

The strict improvement is witnessed by one audited pair orbit in each arm, fair pair randomization, and the legal selector choosing the unique observed member in the requested arm. Then \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key is \(\pi_{00}=1\). For \(M\ge2\), \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) imply \(k_0=k_1=M\). Equation (3) gives \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The former rolling and multinomial charges are \(M\) and \(M+1\), respectively. Only the lower--lower orientation has positive base mass, so \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)=\Theta(M^2)\). The former least-charge dispatcher therefore paid \(\Theta(M)\), while the vertex prepass pays \(\Theta(1)\). This is a statement about the exact key value, not the frontier deficit.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain by \(\texttt{prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary}\). Put \(c=1-b\). Conditional on target orbit \(O_t\), (1) is a binomial strict-prefix count with success probability
\[
\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}.
\]
The calibration orbits are iid and independent of the target by the known randomization, auxiliary-selector, and target assumptions. Averaging gives
\[
\sum_{O_t}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta(O_t))<k_c\}.
\tag{6}
\]
Grouping target orbits by ordered tie class turns (6) into the displayed cumulative-class formula. The lower and upper binomial recurrences, their endpoint clauses, the shorter-tail complexity, and the absence of the other arm's preorder follow exactly from \(\texttt{lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator}\) and \(\texttt{lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator}\). If both ranks equal \(M+1\), both events are certain and the defining positive part is zero.

For binary nonattainment, use four audited pair blocks with fair independent assignment. Let the selector's block probabilities in each arm be
\[
q=(13/20,1/20,1/4,1/20)
\]
and let the common target's block probabilities be
\[
p=(1/20,1/10,1/4,3/5).
\]
With \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), both ranks equal three. Set \(s_0\equiv0\) and order the arm-one blocks as \(1\prec3\prec2\prec4\). The failure probability is
\[
\frac14\left(\frac{13}{20}\right)^3
+\frac1{10}\left(\frac{18}{20}\right)^3
+\frac35\left(\frac{19}{20}\right)^3
=\frac{104957}{160000},
\]
so this four-level frontier objective is \(24957/160000\). For a binary pair, if \(H_a\) is the high-score block set and \(A_a=(1-q(H_a))^3\), inclusion--exclusion gives failure probability
\[
p(H_0)A_0+p(H_1)A_1-p(H_0\cap H_1)A_0A_1.
\tag{7}
\]
In the row order
\[
\varnothing,1,2,3,4,12,13,14,23,24,34,123,124,134,234,1234,
\]
exact substitution in (7), maximizing over the sixteen choices in the other arm, gives the row maxima with common denominator \(320000000\):
\[
\begin{split}
(164616000,&165302000,192052000,198366000,207906936,165912000,164712000,165787368,\\
&203032000,208582976,208582976,164632000,165793875,164631423,202894331,164616000).
\end{split}
\tag{8}
\]
Thus the maximum failure probability in (7) is \(208582976/320000000\), so every binary objective is at most \(759109/5000000\). The difference is
\[
\frac{24957}{160000}-\frac{759109}{5000000}
=\frac{83189}{20000000}>0.
\]
Finally, if target and calibration supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, assign score \(K\) to its target-supported orbits and zero elsewhere, and assign zero in the other arm. The adverse rank event fails surely, so the deficit is at least \(1-\alpha\); the positive-part definition gives the reverse inequality. A boundary arm is certain and reduces to (6). On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), the armwise staircase gives \(k_0=k_1=k\), yielding the symmetric theorem."}

\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\tag{5}
\]
The vertex workspace is constant, and the factorial preprocessing in (5) occurs exactly when the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty. Substitution of (5) into the evaluator's grouping and memo accounting proves both displayed time bounds and the stated \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\) memory bound. Since the construction of each key uses the unchanged full \(R_w\), and its local weight uses the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the prepass and dispatcher preserve calibration-arm dependence, the common target, and the common selector.

The strict improvement is witnessed by one audited pair orbit in each arm, fair pair randomization, and the legal selector choosing the unique observed member in the requested arm. Then \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key is \(\pi_{00}=1\). For \(M\ge2\), \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) imply \(k_0=k_1=M\). Equation (3) gives \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The former rolling and multinomial charges are \(M\) and \(M+1\), respectively. Only the lower--lower orientation has positive base mass, so \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)=\Theta(M^2)\). The former least-charge dispatcher therefore paid \(\Theta(M)\), while the vertex prepass pays \(\Theta(1)\). This is a statement about the exact key value, not the frontier deficit.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain by \(\texttt{prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary}\). Put \(c=1-b\). Conditional on target orbit \(O_t\), (1) is a binomial strict-prefix count with success probability
\[
\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}.
\]
The calibration orbits are iid and independent of the target by the known randomization, auxiliary-selector, and target assumptions. Averaging gives
\[
\sum_{O_t}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta(O_t))<k_c\}.
\tag{6}
\]
Grouping target orbits by ordered tie class turns (6) into the displayed cumulative-class formula. The lower and upper binomial recurrences, their endpoint clauses, the shorter-tail complexity, and the absence of the other arm's preorder follow exactly from \(\texttt{lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator}\) and \(\texttt{lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator}\). If both ranks equal \(M+1\), both events are certain and the defining positive part is zero.

For binary nonattainment, use four audited pair blocks with fair independent assignment. Let the selector's block probabilities in each arm be
\[
q=(13/20,1/20,1/4,1/20)
\]
and let the common target's block probabilities be
\[
p=(1/20,1/10,1/4,3/5).
\]
With \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), both ranks equal three. Set \(s_0\equiv0\) and order the arm-one blocks as \(1\prec3\prec2\prec4\). The failure probability is
\[
\frac14\left(\frac{13}{20}\right)^3
+\frac1{10}\left(\frac{18}{20}\right)^3
+\frac35\left(\frac{19}{20}\right)^3
=\frac{104957}{160000},
\]
so this four-level frontier objective is \(24957/160000\). For a binary pair, if \(H_a\) is the high-score block set and \(A_a=(1-q(H_a))^3\), inclusion--exclusion gives failure probability
\[
p(H_0)A_0+p(H_1)A_1-p(H_0\cap H_1)A_0A_1.
\tag{7}
\]
In the row order
\[
\varnothing,1,2,3,4,12,13,14,23,24,34,123,124,134,234,1234,
\]
exact substitution in (7), maximizing over the sixteen choices in the other arm, gives the row maxima with common denominator \(320000000\):
\[
\begin{split}
(164616000,&165302000,192052000,198366000,207906936,165912000,164712000,165787368,\\
&203032000,208582976,208582976,164632000,165793875,164631423,202894331,164616000).
\end{split}
\tag{8}
\]
Thus the maximum failure probability in (7) is \(208582976/320000000\), so every binary objective is at most \(759109/5000000\). The difference is
\[
\frac{24957}{160000}-\frac{759109}{5000000}
=\frac{83189}{20000000}>0.
\]
Finally, if target and calibration supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, assign score \(K\) to its target-supported orbits and zero elsewhere, and assign zero in the other arm. The adverse rank event fails surely, so the deficit is at least \(1-\alpha\); the positive-part definition gives the reverse inequality. A boundary arm is certain and reduces to (6). On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), the armwise staircase gives \(k_0=k_1=k\), yielding the symmetric theorem."}

{"id":"thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders; every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Retain the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\), local weights \(\omega_C\), exact vectors \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G=|\Pi_\star|\) from \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), \(D_{M,k,k}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k\ \text{and}\ My<k\}\) in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. If \(k\le M\), put
\[
\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each nonvertex exact key \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge evaluator among the rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\), the multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\), and the positive-corner charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), and write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k^2:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
A global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each exact probability once, while differing local weights are applied only to their own preorder objectives. Exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), the uniform bound replaces the two middle sums by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]
\]
and retains the terminal \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\). Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. Every exact key is constructed from unchanged \(R_w\), and every local weight from unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the common target, common selector, and cross-arm dependence are preserved. At \(k=M\), \(\bar k=1\) and the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\). If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).","depends_on":["thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-equivariance","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","prop:disjoint-orbit-witness","prop:rank-boundary","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator"],"status":"proved","justification":"The equal-split result is the exact diagonal of the asymmetric frontier and inherits both coherent probability objects and the branch-adaptive evaluator certificate.","gap":"The coarse diagonal width \(k^2\) misses the reduction \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\), while coefficient evaluation additionally requires a positive chosen corner.","consumer":"Set \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\), compare rolling, multinomial-sum, and positive-corner charges for every exact key, and use branch-conditional memory without altering \(R_w\), \(P_T^C\), or local weights.","proof_tex":"Set \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\). By \(\texttt{lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase}\),
\[
k_0=k_1=k,
\]
and the definition gives \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\). Thus \(\texttt{thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\) applies on the diagonal. Its preorder argument proves exactness and attainment over the bounded orbit-constant score class, with at most \(m_a\) levels, while preserving the coherent \(P_T^C\), the full joint \(R_w\), and the local weights.

Suppose \(k\le M\). For a vertex key \(\pi_{xy}=1\), the diagonal specialization of the deterministic-count identity is
\[
D_{M,k,k}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k\ \text{and}\ My<k\}.
\tag{1}
\]
It costs constant exact time and workspace. Only the nonvertex keys enter the deterministically tie-broken three-way dispatcher, so
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\tag{2}
\]
and the asymmetric charge specializes to
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\tag{3}
\]
The vertex branch adds no nonconstant workspace, and the factorial term in (3) is present if and only if the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty. Substituting (3) and the diagonal workspace \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) into the asymmetric evaluator gives the two displayed time bounds and
\[
O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})
\]
memory. Each exact key still comes from \(R_w\), and each local weight still comes from \(P_T^C\), so computation changes no probability object.

For the strict witness, take the admissible one-orbit-per-arm pair design from the asymmetric theorem. Then \(\pi_{00}=1\). If \(M\ge2\) and \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), the equal split gives \(k=M\), and (1) yields \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior dispatcher paid \(\Theta(M)\), whereas the vertex prepass pays \(\Theta(1)\). If \(k=M+1\), both rank events are certain by \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\), hence \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). The four-pair construction and exact binary enumeration in the asymmetric theorem already lie on the equal-split diagonal, so they give the stated four-level value, binary upper bound, and positive gap. Its disjoint-support construction likewise specializes to \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\) whenever \(k\le M\)."}
