maximality-20 exact simplex edge-key prepass
directive: Extend the exact simplex-vertex prepass into a vertex-and-edge prepass, updating exactly def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass, lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program, lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative, lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator, thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier, and thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier. First evaluate vertices as currently. Next partition the nonvertex support-two keys Pi_edge={pi:|supp(pi)|=2}. For an edge key supported at u=(x0,y0), v=(x1,y1), orient with p=pi_v and N~Bin(M,p), prove R=Mx0+(x1-x0)N and S=My0+(y1-y0)N. Intersect the two strict inequalities R<k0 and S<k1 with 0<=N<=M to derive an explicit integer interval [L(pi),U(pi)] (possibly empty), and evaluate its exact binomial probability using the existing shorter-interval/tail recurrence, deterministic orientation/tie-breaking, exact arithmetic, and O(1) workspace. Partition vertices first, then edges, then dispatch only remaining support>=3 keys among rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum, and positive-corner coefficient branches. Add the exact edge charges to B and B_sym, exclude all edge keys from the three bivariate sums, add no edge term beyond O(1) in the workspace maximum, and preserve the shared factorial preprocessing condition only for keys actually sent to the multinomial branch. Include and prove the strict admissible witness from the audit: M=2n>=4, k0=n, k1=M, pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0), so R=0, S~Bin(M,1/2), D=Pr(S<M)=1-2^{-M}, evaluated in Theta(log M) exact arithmetic and O(1) workspace while the current least-charge nonvertex dispatcher pays Theta(M). Realize it using two audited pair-block orbits, legal equiprobable block selection in arm 1, target in block 2, arm-0 ordering with block 2 lowest and arm-1 ordering block 1 below block 2, with epsilon0=(n+1)/(2n+1), epsilon1=1/(2n+1), alpha=(n+2)/(2n+1). Verify ranks and every audit assumption explicitly. Preserve every exact key, R_w, P_T^C, local weights, common target, common selector, strict-prefix convention, active-arm/boundary/pair/empty-library/weighted semantics. Synchronize tldr, project_justification gap/niche/fill, related_work, abstract, introduction, Interpretation, conclusion, resource formulas, technical limitation, honest scope, tables, consumer guidance, and matching core prose to say vertex-and-edge prepasses followed by the three-way support>=3 dispatcher, without suppressing the active-arm certificate.

changed.from:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). For \((x,y)\in\{0,1\}^2\), define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). Let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\). If both ranks are at most \(M\), then the recurrence \(d_0(0,0)=1\) and \(d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)\), with out-of-range entries zero and only \(0\le r<k_0\), \(0\le s<k_1\) retained, satisfies \[\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)=\Pr\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\quad \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\}.\] After \(R\) is tabulated, the four cells and recurrence require \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) arithmetic operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. If exactly one rank equals \(M+1\), the corresponding event is certain and a one-dimensional recurrence for the other count requires \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory, where \(k_c\le M\) is the other rank. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint event has probability one and requires no recurrence. For any finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then the two terminal counts are \(Mx\) and \(My\) surely, so
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}.
\]
Thus the simplex-vertex prepass evaluates each such key in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace before any recurrence; only nonvertex two-finite-rank keys require a generic exact evaluator.
changed.to:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). Define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). For \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\), if both ranks are at most \(M\), the retained recurrence
\[
d_0(0,0)=1,\qquad
d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)
\]
satisfies
\[
\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)
=\Pr\!\left\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\ \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\right\}.
\]
After \(R\) is tabulated, this generic recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. With exactly one boundary rank, the other one-dimensional recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory; with two boundary ranks the event is certain.

For a finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, first evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\}\) by
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}
\]
at its unique support point \((x,y)\). Next evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\notin\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}\) as follows. For a deterministically oriented support \(\{u,v\}\), put \(p=\pi_v\), \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), \(c_a=Mu_a\), and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\). Then the two terminal counts are
\[
R_M=c_0+d_0N,\qquad S_M=c_1+d_1N,
\]
and their strict inequalities are equivalent to the explicit interval \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\), with the stated empty-interval convention. Hence
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
The vertex branch costs \(O(1)\) exact time and workspace; the edge branch costs \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace. Deterministic orientation, evaluator selection, and tie-breaking preserve exactness. Only two-finite-rank keys with support size at least three require a generic bivariate evaluator.
changed.reason: The current generic-dispatch sentence leaves all nonvertices to bivariate evaluation. The corrected claim narrows that generic domain to support-at-least-three keys by proving an exact support-two affine-binomial interval evaluator.

changed.from:
Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi=(\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10},\pi_{11})\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) independent draws with this law, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
An empty inner range contributes zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for every positive integer \(j\). If
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
then \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). After probability-independent factorials through \(M!\) are precomputed once, one key \(\pi\) is evaluated by rebuilding its four power tables in \(O(M)\) operations and then summing the feasible terms in \(O(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) operations, using \(O(M)\) auxiliary memory. The rolling two-count recurrence is an exact alternative using \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) auxiliary memory. A time-selecting hybrid therefore uses \(O(\min\{M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1},Mk_0k_1\})\) operations per key, conditional memory \(O(M)\) on the multinomial branch and \(O(k_0k_1)\) on the recurrence branch, and unconditional auxiliary memory \(O(\max\{M,k_0k_1\})\). For a finite set \(\Pi\) of keys processed sequentially, define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
If \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) and \(\pi_{xy}=1\), then both counts are deterministic and
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\},
\]
which is evaluable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Partition only the nonvertex keys between the two generic branches,
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}.
\]
The exact aggregate resource charge is
\[
O\!\left(|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1\right)
\]
operations. The \(O(M)\) factorial preprocessing is shared once if and only if the nonvertex multinomial branch is nonempty; four probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\). Sequential processing and retention of one scalar answer per key use \(O(|\Pi|+\max\{1,M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\})\) memory. The vertex prepass precedes the corner-complemented three-way dispatcher in downstream use, so vertex keys enter neither a rolling, multinomial-sum, nor positive-corner coefficient branch.
changed.to:
Let \(M\ge1\), \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) iid draws, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Empty ranges contribute zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for \(j>0\). With
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r<k_0}\sum_{s<k_1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
one has \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). Shared factorial preprocessing costs \(O(M)\); a multinomial key costs \(O(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) time and \(O(M)\) memory, while the rolling alternative costs \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) time and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory.

For a finite key set \(\Pi\), first partition
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}},
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, respectively, and every key in the latter two strata has support size at least three. Vertices use the deterministic count formula. Edges use the exact binomial interval formula and charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\). Only the support-at-least-three keys are assigned between the multinomial and rolling branches. Sequential processing has aggregate operation charge
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})
+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1
\right).
\]
The factorial table is built if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\), probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each multinomial key, and retention of one scalar per key uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi|+\max\{1,\ M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},\
k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\}
\right)
\]
memory. The vertex-and-edge prepass precedes the downstream three-way dispatcher, so neither stratum enters a bivariate rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner coefficient branch.
changed.reason: The aggregate resource statement must remove support-two keys before either generic branch and charge their one-dimensional exact evaluation separately.

changed.from:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Define
\[
\kappa_{w,a}(O\mid z)=\sum_{v:(v,a)\in O}\kappa_{w,a}(v\mid z),\qquad R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z).
\]
For \(v\in\mathcal V\) and \(a\in\{0,1\}\), let \(O_a(v)\) be the orbit containing \((v,a)\), and define
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\},\qquad L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|.
\]
Then \(L\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For a preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\) with ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_{a,1}\prec_a\cdots\prec_a C_{a,d_a}\), let
\[
P_T^C(j,\ell)=\sum_{\substack{O_0,O_1\\c_0(O_0)=j,\ c_1(O_1)=\ell}}P_T(O_0,O_1),\qquad L_C=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)|.
\]
Then \(L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\). One two-dimensional prefix table of the unchanged full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\) yields the exact four-cell vector \(\pi^C(j,\ell)=(\pi_{00}^C,\pi_{01}^C,\pi_{10}^C,\pi_{11}^C)\) in constant time for every supported target class pair. Define
\[
\Pi_C=\{\pi^C(j,\ell):(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\},\qquad U_C=|\Pi_C|\le L_C,
\]
and retain the local coherent weights
\[
\omega_C(\pi)=\sum_{\substack{(j,\ell)\in\operatorname{supp}(P_T^C)\\\pi^C(j,\ell)=\pi}}P_T^C(j,\ell).
\]
The preorder objective is \(\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\), where the coupled two-count recurrence value depends only on \(M\), \(k_0\), \(k_1\), and \(\pi\). Let \(\mathfrak C\) be the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs and define
\[
\Pi_\star=\bigcup_{C\in\mathfrak C}\Pi_C,\qquad G=|\Pi_\star|.
\]
Define the simplex-vertex stratum
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), the exact value is \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\), computable in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace.
A global cross-preorder memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Differing local weights \(\omega_C(\pi)\) never enter the recurrence and are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Hence the memo preserves the one common target through the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), the full within-replicate cross-arm calibration dependence through the unchanged \(R_w\), and the common selector \(w\). Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among: the corner-oriented rolling branch with charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum branch with charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\); and the positive-corner coefficient branch with charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Break ties prespecifically, and let
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+
\]
be the resulting exact-key partition. For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) be a minimizing positive-base orientation in \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), with oriented thresholds \(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where every empty sum is zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
Thus empty-branch maxima are harmless. A deterministic local balanced tree uses \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic for preorder \(C\). The global balanced-tree memo adds \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\) exact comparisons and evaluates only the \(G\) distinct keys. After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing charge and is absent when \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}=\varnothing\). If \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right).
\]
The working-memory bound, including all non-recurrence tables, the current local exact-key table, and the \(G\) memoized scalar answers, is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). In particular, if \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and \(\mathsf C_{M,M,M}=M\), so the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).
changed.to:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Define the arm-orbit sets \(\mathcal O_a\), their sizes \(m_a\), the joint calibration-orbit law
\[
R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
and the coherent common-target law
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)
\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\}.
\]
Let \(L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For every preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), the strict-prefix keys \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), and the local weights \(\omega_C\) be the corresponding pushforwards. Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), and
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events under }C\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi).
\]
A global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\); the fourth cell is determined by normalization, and local weights never enter the key. Thus the unchanged \(R_w\), unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), common selector, common target, and within-replicate cross-arm dependence are preserved.

Let \(\Pi_\star=\bigcup_C\Pi_C\), \(G=|\Pi_\star|\), and partition it first into the support-one stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), then the support-two stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), and then the support-at-least-three strata \(\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}},\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}},\Pi_+\) chosen by deterministic least-charge comparison. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad
\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}
\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Vertices use the deterministic formula; edges use the exact binomial interval charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\); and only support-at-least-three keys enter the rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial, or positive-corner branches. With prespecified tie-breaking,
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
If \(\sigma(\pi)\) minimizes the positive-corner charge, define
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(
\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{\min(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}):\pi\in\Pi_+\}
\right).
\]
Vertices and edges contribute only \(O(1)\) workspace, and the factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\). With \(\mathfrak C\) the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration after \(R_w\) is tabulated costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
and its uniform version replaces the middle preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}].
\]
Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\).

There is a strict admissible edge improvement. Let \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
The edge evaluator uses \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the least-charge former nonvertex dispatcher uses \(\Theta(M)\) arithmetic. This key is realized by two audited pair blocks under \(S_2^2\), independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, a singleton selector library whose legal equivariant arm-\(1\) rule selects either block equiprobably and then its unique observed arm-\(1\) member, a target supported in block \(2\), an arm-\(0\) preorder with block \(2\) lowest, and an arm-\(1\) preorder with block \(1\) below block \(2\). Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.
changed.reason: The global memo and resource certificate must add the edge stratum, exclude it from all three bivariate sums, and include the audited strict improvement.

changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps for armwise ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) denote the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. If \(k_0,k_1\le M\), the maximum is attained by enumerating preorder pairs, and every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Let \(P_T\), \(L\), \(R_w\), \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), \(\omega_C\), \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G\) be as in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0\ \text{and}\ My<k_1\}\) in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. Thus \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), the global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), and \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\). Each exact four-cell probability is computed once globally, while the differing local coherent weights are multiplied only when each preorder objective is formed. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge exact evaluator among the corner-oriented rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\), the zero-cell-safe multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\), and the positive-corner coefficient charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). With prespecified tie-breaking, write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
Deterministic local grouping costs \(O(L_C\log(1+L_C))\) exact comparisons and \(O(L_C)\) aggregation arithmetic, and global lookup costs \(O((\sum_CU_C)\log(1+G))\). After \(R_w\) is tabulated, exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right).
\]
The leading \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), a uniform bound is
\[
O\!\left(|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]+\mathsf B\right),
\]
and working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). Every \(\pi\) is constructed from the unchanged full \(R_w\), while every \(\omega_C\) is derived from the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\); hence memoization and branch selection preserve calibration-arm dependence, the one common target, and the common selector. If \(k_0=k_1=M\), then \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=1\) and the rolling per-key charge is \(\Theta(M)\), rather than the coarse \(\Theta(M^3)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), put \(c=1-b\), so \(k_c\le M\). For a fixed nonboundary score map \(s_c\), define \(\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O\in\mathcal O_c:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\). The exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\bigl(M,\theta(O_t)\bigr)<k_c\right\}.
\]
For ordered nonempty tie classes \(C_1\prec\cdots\prec C_d\), define \(q_j=q_c(C_j)\), \(p_j=p_c(C_j)\), and \(\theta_j=\sum_{\ell<j}q_\ell\); then the probability is \(\sum_{j=1}^d p_j\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta_j)<k_c\}\). These masses are formed by one cumulative ordered-class scan. For \(0<\theta<1\), exact lower-tail terms start at \(t_0=(1-\theta)^M\) and obey
\[
t_{r+1}=t_r\frac{M-r}{r+1}\frac{\theta}{1-\theta},\qquad 0\le r<k_c-1,
\]
whereas exact upper-tail terms start at \(u_M=\theta^M\) and obey
\[
u_{r-1}=u_r\frac{r}{M-r+1}\frac{1-\theta}{\theta},\qquad k_c<r\le M.
\]
Exponentiation by squaring initializes either endpoint in \(O(\log M)\) operations, and the shorter tail uses \(O(\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\})\) further operations. At \(\theta=0\) and \(\theta=1\), the probabilities are \(1\) and \(0\), respectively, without division. After \(q_c\) and \(p_c\) are tabulated, exact maximization enumerates only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and uses
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c\left[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}\right]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory, with no factor \(F_{m_{1-c}}\), \(|\mathcal V|\), or per-case \(m_0m_1\) scan. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), evaluation is constant and the frontier is zero. Binary attainment is false: in the admissible symmetric instance \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective equals \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a gap of \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in arm \(a\) and \(k_a\le M\), then \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=1-\alpha\). If \(k_a=M+1\), the frontier reduces exactly to the other arm's rank event through the preceding one-arm formula. On the diagonal \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\), this is \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\).
changed.to:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps for ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. If both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two and the last three contain only support-at-least-three keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\).

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the frontier is \(1-\alpha\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.
changed.reason: The finite-rank frontier evaluator must use vertex and edge prepasses before dispatching only support-at-least-three keys, with synchronized time, memory, and audit claims.

changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) in \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over the stated class of bounded orbit-constant score maps; no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. Write \(\mathcal O_a=\{O\in\mathcal O:O\subseteq\mathcal U_a\}\), \(m_a=|\mathcal O_a|\), and let \(F_m\) be the number of total preorders on an \(m\)-element labelled set. Its maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) pairs of total preorders; every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. Retain the unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), full joint calibration-orbit law \(R_w\), local weights \(\omega_C\), exact vectors \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and \(G=|\Pi_\star|\) from \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) with \(\pi_{xy}=1\), \(D_{M,k,k}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k\ \text{and}\ My<k\}\) in \(O(1)\) exact time and \(O(1)\) workspace. If \(k\le M\), put
\[
\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\},\qquad \mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Evaluate every \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) by the deterministic simplex-vertex formula. For each nonvertex exact key \(\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), deterministically select a least-charge evaluator among the rolling charge \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\), the multinomial-sum charge \(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\), and the positive-corner charge \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\), and write
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_+\), let \(\sigma(\pi)\) attain \(\mathsf C_+(\pi)\). Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k}\right)+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
where empty sums are zero, and
\[
\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{\bar k^2:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\cup\left\{\min\{t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}\}:\pi\in\Pi_+\right\}\right).
\]
The vertex branch contributes only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, which is absorbed by the constant term in this maximum; the factorial term is present if and only if the nonvertex set \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\) is nonempty.
A global memo keyed exactly by \((M,k,k,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\), with \(\pi_{11}=1-\pi_{00}-\pi_{01}-\pi_{10}\), computes each exact probability once, while differing local weights are applied only to their own preorder objectives. Exact enumeration uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}\left[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C\right]+\left\{\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}U_C\right\}\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right).
\]
With \(L_{\max}=\max_C L_C\) and \(U_{\max}=\max_C U_C\), the uniform bound replaces the two middle sums by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\left[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}\right]
\]
and retains the terminal \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\). Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). The term \(M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}\) is exactly one shared factorial-preprocessing term. Every exact key is constructed from unchanged \(R_w\), and every local weight from unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), so the common target, common selector, and cross-arm dependence are preserved. At \(k=M\), \(\bar k=1\) and the rolling per-key charge improves from \(\Theta(M^3)\) to \(\Theta(M)\), with the positive-corner orientation potentially smaller. In the admissible one-orbit-per-arm instance, \(R_w\) and \(P_T\) are point masses and the unique strict-prefix key satisfies \(\pi_{00}=1\). For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) and the symmetric allocation \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/(M+1)\) gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1\). The prior least-charge three-way dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\) on this key, whereas the vertex prepass costs \(\Theta(1)\). If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false: in an admissible four-pair instance with \(M=3\) and \(\alpha=1/2\), a four-level ordering has frontier objective \(24957/160000\), whereas every binary score pair has objective at most \(759109/5000000\), a strict gap of \(83189/20000000\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\).
changed.to:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps, with no asserted equality to a supremum over all invariant latent laws. It is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the last three strata contain only support-at-least-three keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices and edges add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a support-at-least-three key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\).

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.
changed.reason: The equal-split theorem must inherit the vertex-and-edge partition and its corrected diagonal resource certificate.

changed.from:
{"id":"def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","name":"simplex-vertex exact-key prepass","construction":"For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:\max_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}=1\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \((x_\pi,y_\pi)\) be its unique unit-mass cell and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx_\pi<k_0,\ My_\pi<k_1\}\). Only keys in \(\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\) enter the remaining exact dispatcher.","inputs":["\(\Pi_\star\)","\(M\)","\(k_0\)","\(k_1\)"]}
changed.to:
{"id":"def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","name":"simplex vertex-and-edge exact-key prepass","construction":"For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace. Only keys in \(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}})\), all of which have support size at least three, enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.","inputs":["\(\Pi_\star\)","\(M\)","\(k_0\)","\(k_1\)"]}
changed.reason: The exact-key prepass now evaluates simplex vertices first and all remaining support-two edge laws second by a one-dimensional affine-binomial interval; only support-at-least-three laws enter the bivariate dispatcher.

note: Codex accepted the vertex-and-edge definition replacement and all five statement changes with superseding proofs. The asymmetric result-class warning was confirmed false positive; exactness, attainment, binary nonattainment, sharp witness, boundaries, and diagonal survive.
maximality-21 incompatible zero-corner face prepass
directive: Add an oriented zero-corner incompatibility prepass after vertex and support-two edge prepasses and before the remaining bivariate dispatcher, updating exactly def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass, lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator, lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative, lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program, lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator, thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier, and thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier. For a remaining support-three key, inspect the existing LL/LU/UL/UU corner orientations. If an orientation sigma has pi^sigma_11=0 and transformed upper-tail thresholds t^sigma_0+t^sigma_1>M, prove T_0+T_1<=M, so the failure events {T_0>=t^sigma_0} and {T_1>=t^sigma_1} are disjoint and J_sigma=1-Pr(T_0>=t^sigma_0)-Pr(T_1>=t^sigma_1). Evaluate both marginals by exact shorter binomial-tail recurrences, then reconstruct the original lower-lower probability with the existing orientation identities, adding only nonduplicated marginal reconstruction costs. Give deterministic orientation/tie-breaking. Charge O(log M+min(t_0,M-t_0+1)+min(t_1,M-t_1+1)) exact arithmetic plus only necessary nonduplicated reconstruction marginals and O(1) workspace. Create a distinct memo partition stratum for these face keys; exclude it from rolling, multinomial, and positive-corner sums, do not trigger factorial preprocessing, and add no nonconstant workspace term. Preserve vertices, edges, remaining support>=3 dispatcher, exact keys and weights, R_w, P_T^C, common target/selector, strict prefixes, all statistical/boundary/active-arm/pair/empty-library/weighted semantics. Prove the strict admissible witness for every M>=2: k0=k1=M and pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0), yielding D=1-2*3^{-M}; the new branch costs Theta(log M), whereas rolling, multinomial, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost Theta(M). Realize the key using two pair blocks, the trivial finite group, known assignment law uniform on three one-treated-per-pair assignment vectors, deterministic legal observable selector lookups producing strict-prefix pairs 00,01,10, singleton common selector library, fixed common target in block 2, and armwise preorders putting block-1 singleton orbits strictly below the target. Verify every legality and rank detail. Synchronize tldr, project gap/niche/fill, related_work, abstract, introduction, interpretation, conclusion, technical limitation, honest scope, dispatcher partitions, resource formulas, tables, consumer guidance, and both frontier statements/proofs without suppressing the active-arm contribution.

changed.from:
Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For iid \((B_{i0},B_{i1})\sim\pi\), put \(R=\sum_iB_{i0}\), \(S=\sum_iB_{i1}\), \(h_a=M-k_a+1\), and \(\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\}\). For \(\sigma_a\in\{L,U\}\), replace \(B_{ia}\) by itself under \(L\) and by \(1-B_{ia}\) under \(U\), set \(t_a=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a=h_a\) under \(U\), and let \(J_{\sigma_0\sigma_1}\) be the probability that both transformed counts are strictly below their thresholds. A rolling lower-left recurrence for the correspondingly permuted four-cell law computes \(J_{\sigma_0\sigma_1}\) in
\[
O\!\left(\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{t_0,i+1\}\min\{t_1,i+1\}\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(t_0t_1)\) memory. Moreover, \(\Pr\{R<k_0,S<k_1\}\) equals \(J_{LL}\), \(\Pr\{R<k_0\}-J_{LU}\), \(\Pr\{S<k_1\}-J_{UL}\), or \(1-\Pr\{R\ge k_0\}-\Pr\{S\ge k_1\}+J_{UU}\), respectively. Choosing each orientation to minimize \(t_a\), and evaluating required binomial marginals exactly, yields time
\[
O\!\left(\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}\right)
\]
and recurrence workspace \(O(\bar k_0\bar k_1)\).

Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For iid \((B_{i0},B_{i1})\sim\pi\), put \(R=\sum_iB_{i0}\), \(S=\sum_iB_{i1}\), \(h_a=M-k_a+1\), and \(\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\}\). For \(\sigma_a\in\{L,U\}\), replace \(B_{ia}\) by itself under \(L\) and by \(1-B_{ia}\) under \(U\), set \(t_a=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a=h_a\) under \(U\), and let \(J_{\sigma_0\sigma_1}\) be the probability that both transformed counts are strictly below their thresholds. A rolling lower-left recurrence for the correspondingly permuted four-cell law computes \(J_{\sigma_0\sigma_1}\) in
\[
O\!\left(\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{t_0,i+1\}\min\{t_1,i+1\}\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(t_0t_1)\) memory. Moreover, \(\Pr\{R<k_0,S<k_1\}\) equals \(J_{LL}\), \(\Pr\{R<k_0\}-J_{LU}\), \(\Pr\{S<k_1\}-J_{UL}\), or \(1-\Pr\{R\ge k_0\}-\Pr\{S\ge k_1\}+J_{UU}\), respectively. Choosing each orientation to minimize \(t_a\), and evaluating required binomial marginals exactly, yields time
\[
O\!\left(\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}\right)
\]
and recurrence workspace \(O(\bar k_0\bar k_1)\).

For a support-three law, write \(\pi^\sigma\) for the four-cell law after orientation \(\sigma\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the corresponding transformed terminal counts, and use the thresholds \(t_a^\sigma\) above. Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\left\{\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2:
\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\quad t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\right\}.
\]
For every \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), one has \(T_0^\sigma+T_1^\sigma\le M\) almost surely, the two events \(\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}\) and \(\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}\) are disjoint, and therefore
\[
J_\sigma
=1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Put
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=
1+\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\}.
\]
Each marginal tail is evaluated by the exact shorter binomial-tail recurrence. The marginal quantities required by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) reconstruction identity are complements of tails already evaluated for \(J_\sigma\), so no duplicated marginal recurrence is charged. Deterministic charge minimization followed by lexicographic tie-breaking therefore evaluates the original lower--lower probability in \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.
changed.reason: The prior resource claim leaves a support-three zero-corner incompatibility inside the bivariate evaluator. The corrected claim narrows that residual domain by deriving the exact disjoint-tail face branch.

changed.from:
Let \(M\ge1\), \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) iid draws, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Empty ranges contribute zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for \(j>0\). With
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r<k_0}\sum_{s<k_1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
one has \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). Shared factorial preprocessing costs \(O(M)\); a multinomial key costs \(O(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) time and \(O(M)\) memory, while the rolling alternative costs \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) time and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory.

For a finite key set \(\Pi\), first partition
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}},
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, respectively, and every key in the latter two strata has support size at least three. Vertices use the deterministic count formula. Edges use the exact binomial interval formula and charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\). Only the support-at-least-three keys are assigned between the multinomial and rolling branches. Sequential processing has aggregate operation charge
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})
+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1
\right).
\]
The factorial table is built if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\), probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each multinomial key, and retention of one scalar per key uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi|+\max\{1,\ M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},\
k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\}
\right)
\]
memory. The vertex-and-edge prepass precedes the downstream three-way dispatcher, so neither stratum enters a bivariate rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner coefficient branch.

For a finite key set \(\Pi\), first partition
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}},
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, respectively, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains exactly the remaining support-three keys admitted by the oriented zero-corner incompatibility prepass, and every key in the last two strata has support at least three and is not a face key. Vertices use the deterministic count formula. Edges use the exact binomial interval formula and charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\). Face keys use the exact charge \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator}\). Only the nonface support-at-least-three keys are assigned between the multinomial and rolling branches. Sequential processing has aggregate operation charge
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})
+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1
\right).
\]
The factorial table is built if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\), probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each multinomial key, and retention of one scalar per key uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi|+\max\{1,\ M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},\
k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\}
\right)
\]
memory. The vertex, edge, and oriented zero-corner face prepasses precede the downstream three-way dispatcher, so none of those strata enters a bivariate rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner coefficient branch. Face keys use only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace and do not trigger factorial preprocessing.
changed.reason: The old partition sends every support-at-least-three key to a generic branch. The corrected partition removes exactly the eligible support-three face keys and charges them separately.

changed.from:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). Define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). For \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\), if both ranks are at most \(M\), the retained recurrence
\[
d_0(0,0)=1,\qquad
d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)
\]
satisfies
\[
\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)
=\Pr\!\left\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\ \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\right\}.
\]
After \(R\) is tabulated, this generic recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. With exactly one boundary rank, the other one-dimensional recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory; with two boundary ranks the event is certain.

For a finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, first evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\}\) by
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}
\]
at its unique support point \((x,y)\). Next evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\notin\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}\) as follows. For a deterministically oriented support \(\{u,v\}\), put \(p=\pi_v\), \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), \(c_a=Mu_a\), and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\). Then the two terminal counts are
\[
R_M=c_0+d_0N,\qquad S_M=c_1+d_1N,
\]
and their strict inequalities are equivalent to the explicit interval \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\), with the stated empty-interval convention. Hence
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
The vertex branch costs \(O(1)\) exact time and workspace; the edge branch costs \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace. Deterministic orientation, evaluator selection, and tie-breaking preserve exactness. Only two-finite-rank keys with support size at least three require a generic bivariate evaluator.
changed.to:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). Define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). For \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\), if both ranks are at most \(M\), the retained recurrence
\[
d_0(0,0)=1,\qquad
d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)
\]
satisfies
\[

=\Pr\!\left\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\ \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\right\}.
\]
After \(R\) is tabulated, this generic recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. With exactly one boundary rank, the other one-dimensional recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory; with two boundary ranks the event is certain.

For a finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, first evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\}\) by
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}
\]
at its unique support point \((x,y)\). Next evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\notin\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}\) as follows. For a deterministically oriented support \(\{u,v\}\), put \(p=\pi_v\), \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), \(c_a=Mu_a\), and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\). Then the two terminal counts are
\[
R_M=c_0+d_0N,\qquad S_M=c_1+d_1N,
\]
and their strict inequalities are equivalent to the explicit interval \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\), with the stated empty-interval convention. Hence
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
The vertex branch costs \(O(1)\) exact time and workspace; the edge branch costs \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace. Deterministic orientation, evaluator selection, and tie-breaking preserve exactness. For every remaining support-three key, inspect the four corner orientations. If \(\pi^\sigma_{11}=0\) and \(t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\), then \(T_0^\sigma+T_1^\sigma\le M\), so the two transformed failure events are disjoint and
\[
J_\sigma=1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
-\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
The exact shorter binomial-tail recurrences and the existing corner identities evaluate the original lower--lower probability in \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace; reconstruction reuses complements of the two evaluated tails. Deterministic charge minimization and lexicographic tie-breaking preserve exactness. Only two-finite-rank support-at-least-three keys outside this face prepass can require a generic bivariate evaluator.
changed.reason: The generic-evaluator domain is too broad: an oriented support-three zero-corner face with incompatible thresholds reduces to two marginal tails. The corrected statement restricts generic bivariate evaluation to the nonface remainder.

changed.from:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Define the arm-orbit sets \(\mathcal O_a\), their sizes \(m_a\), the joint calibration-orbit law
\[
R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
and the coherent common-target law
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)
\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\}.
\]
Let \(L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For every preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), the strict-prefix keys \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), and the local weights \(\omega_C\) be the corresponding pushforwards. Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), and
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events under }C\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi).
\]
A global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\); the fourth cell is determined by normalization, and local weights never enter the key. Thus the unchanged \(R_w\), unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), common selector, common target, and within-replicate cross-arm dependence are preserved.

Let \(\Pi_\star=\bigcup_C\Pi_C\), \(G=|\Pi_\star|\), and partition it first into the support-one stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), then the support-two stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), and then the support-at-least-three strata \(\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}},\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}},\Pi_+\) chosen by deterministic least-charge comparison. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad
\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}
\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Vertices use the deterministic formula; edges use the exact binomial interval charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\); and only support-at-least-three keys enter the rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial, or positive-corner branches. With prespecified tie-breaking,
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
If \(\sigma(\pi)\) minimizes the positive-corner charge, define
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(
\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{\min(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}):\pi\in\Pi_+\}
\right).
\]
Vertices and edges contribute only \(O(1)\) workspace, and the factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\). With \(\mathfrak C\) the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration after \(R_w\) is tabulated costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
and its uniform version replaces the middle preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}].
\]
Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\).

There is a strict admissible edge improvement. Let \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
The edge evaluator uses \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the least-charge former nonvertex dispatcher uses \(\Theta(M)\) arithmetic. This key is realized by two audited pair blocks under \(S_2^2\), independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, a singleton selector library whose legal equivariant arm-\(1\) rule selects either block equiprobably and then its unique observed arm-\(1\) member, a target supported in block \(2\), an arm-\(0\) preorder with block \(2\) lowest, and an arm-\(1\) preorder with block \(1\) below block \(2\). Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Vertices use the deterministic formula; edges use the exact binomial interval charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\); eligible support-three face keys use \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\); and only the remaining nonface support-at-least-three keys enter the rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial, or positive-corner branches. With prespecified tie-breaking,
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
If \(\sigma(\pi)\) minimizes the positive-corner charge, define
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(
\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{\min(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}):\pi\in\Pi_+\}
\right).
\]
Vertices, edges, and face keys contribute only \(O(1)\) workspace, and the factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\). The face stratum contributes no nonconstant term to \(\mathsf W\). With \(\mathfrak C\) the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration after \(R_w\) is tabulated costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
and its uniform version replaces the middle preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}].
\]
Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\).

There is a strict admissible edge improvement. Let \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
The edge evaluator uses \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the least-charge former nonvertex dispatcher uses \(\Theta(M)\) arithmetic. This key is realized by two audited pair blocks under \(S_2^2\), independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, a singleton selector library whose legal equivariant arm-\(1\) rule selects either block equiprobably and then its unique observed arm-\(1\) member, a target supported in block \(2\), an arm-\(0\) preorder with block \(2\) lowest, and an arm-\(1\) preorder with block \(1\) below block \(2\). Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is also a strict admissible zero-corner face improvement for every \(M\ge2\). Let \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The \(LL\) face orientation has \(\pi_{11}=0\) and \(t_0+t_1=2M>M\), so the face evaluator costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace. The rolling, multinomial, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation each cost \(\Theta(M)\). The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial finite group, a known law uniform on three assignment vectors having one treated member per block, deterministic legal observable selector lookups whose strict-prefix pairs are \(00\), \(01\), and \(10\), a singleton common selector library, and a fixed common target in block \(2\). In each arm, place both block-\(1\) singleton orbits strictly below the target orbit and tie the block-\(2\) singleton orbits with the target. Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.
changed.reason: The five-way memo partition is too broad because eligible support-three face keys need no bivariate dispatch. The corrected statement isolates that stratum and sharpens the same exact evaluator without changing its probability objects.

arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

where the last three strata contain only support-at-least-three keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and

Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[

+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}

&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices and edges add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)

changed.from:
For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace. Only keys in \(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}})\), all of which have support size at least three, enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.

For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-three key and every corner orientation \(\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2\), let \(\pi^\sigma\) be the transformed four-cell law and let \(t_a^\sigma=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a^\sigma=M-k_a+1\) under \(U\). Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[
J_\sigma=
1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace. Only keys in
\(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}})\)
enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.
changed.reason: The current exact-key prepass stops after vertices and support-two edges. The corrected construction adds the directed oriented zero-corner incompatibility prepass, its exact marginal-tail charge, deterministic tie-breaking, and exclusion from every later bivariate branch.

note: Codex accepted all seven zero-corner face-prepass changes atomically with six superseding proofs and synchronized prose.
maximality-22 rank-one support-four prepass
directive: Add a proved lemma lem:rank-one-four-cell-rank-evaluator and a rank-one support-four exact prepass after vertices, edges, and incompatible zero-corner faces and before rolling/multinomial/positive-corner evaluation. Update exactly that new lemma plus def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass, lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator, lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative, lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program, lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator, thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier, and thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier. Test the exact memoized key equality pi00*pi11=pi01*pi10. For a support-four PMF satisfying it, define theta0=pi10+pi11 and theta1=pi01+pi11 and prove the generating function factors as [(1-theta0)+theta0 x][(1-theta1)+theta1 y]; its Mth power factors, so R~Bin(M,theta0), S~Bin(M,theta1) independently and D=H(M,k0,theta0)H(M,k1,theta1), with H the strict binomial CDF. Evaluate both factors with lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator in O(log M+bar k0+bar k1) exact arithmetic and O(1) workspace, deterministic exact testing and no approximation. Add a distinct rank-one memo partition, exclude it from all three remaining bivariate sums, do not trigger factorial preprocessing, and add no nonconstant workspace term. Preserve all earlier vertex/edge/face strata, exact keys, R_w, P_T^C, local weights, common selector/target, strict prefixes, and every statistical/boundary/pair/empty-library/weighted/active-arm claim. Prove the strict admissible witness from the audit: M=2n, n>=3, k0=n, k1=n+2, epsilon0=(n+1)/(2n+1), epsilon1=(n-1)/(2n+1), alpha=2n/(2n+1); two pair blocks, trivial group, independent fair one-treated-per-block assignments, singleton selector choosing the unique control in block 1 for arm 0 and unique treated member in block 2 for arm 1, target v_{2,0}, and armwise preorders making one block-1 control orbit and the block-2 mate-treatment orbit strictly below the target orbits. Verify this legally yields pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4) and D=H(2n,n,1/2)H(2n,n+2,1/2). Prove the new cost is Theta(M) and constant workspace while every previously eligible branch has least Theta(M^2) time and Theta(M) workspace. Synchronize tldr, project gap/niche/fill, related_work, abstract, introduction, interpretation, conclusion, technical limitation, honest scope, all dispatcher/resource formulas, tables, consumer guidance, and matching core fields without suppressing the active-arm contribution.

Let \(M\ge1\), let \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For iid \((B_{i0},B_{i1})\sim\pi\), put \(R=\sum_iB_{i0}\), \(S=\sum_iB_{i1}\), \(h_a=M-k_a+1\), and \(\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\}\). For \(\sigma_a\in\{L,U\}\), replace \(B_{ia}\) by itself under \(L\) and by \(1-B_{ia}\) under \(U\), set \(t_a=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a=h_a\) under \(U\), and let \(J_{\sigma_0\sigma_1}\) be the probability that both transformed counts are strictly below their thresholds. A rolling lower-left recurrence for the correspondingly permuted four-cell law computes \(J_{\sigma_0\sigma_1}\) in
\[
O\!\left(\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{t_0,i+1\}\min\{t_1,i+1\}\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(t_0t_1)\) memory. Moreover, \(\Pr\{R<k_0,S<k_1\}\) equals \(J_{LL}\), \(\Pr\{R<k_0\}-J_{LU}\), \(\Pr\{S<k_1\}-J_{UL}\), or \(1-\Pr\{R\ge k_0\}-\Pr\{S\ge k_1\}+J_{UU}\), respectively. Choosing each orientation to minimize \(t_a\), and evaluating required binomial marginals exactly, yields time
\[
O\!\left(\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}\right)
\]
and recurrence workspace \(O(\bar k_0\bar k_1)\).

For a support-three law, write \(\pi^\sigma\) for the four-cell law after orientation \(\sigma\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the corresponding transformed terminal counts, and use the thresholds \(t_a^\sigma\) above. Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\left\{\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2:
\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\quad t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\right\}.
\]
For every \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), one has \(T_0^\sigma+T_1^\sigma\le M\) almost surely, the two events \(\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}\) and \(\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}\) are disjoint, and therefore
\[
J_\sigma
=1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Put
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=
1+\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\}.
\]
Each marginal tail is evaluated by the exact shorter binomial-tail recurrence. The marginal quantities required by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) reconstruction identity are complements of tails already evaluated for \(J_\sigma\), so no duplicated marginal recurrence is charged. Deterministic charge minimization followed by lexicographic tie-breaking therefore evaluates the original lower--lower probability in \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.
