For every remaining support-four key satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), define \(\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11}\), \(\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11}\), \(\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\}\), and \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1\). Exact determinant testing and the factorization
\[
\pi_{00}+\pi_{10}x+\pi_{01}y+\pi_{11}xy
=\{(1-\theta_0)+\theta_0x\}\{(1-\theta_1)+\theta_1y\}
\]
give
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1)
\]
in \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace. Such a key is evaluated after the vertex, edge, and incompatible-face strata and before any rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch.
changed.reason: The residual bivariate dispatcher currently includes full-support determinant-zero keys even though exact factorization gives a distinct constant-workspace product-binomial prepass. The proposed claim narrows the residual dispatcher to keys outside that exact stratum and records the resulting valid resource improvement without changing the probability object.

changed.from:
Let \(M\ge1\), \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M\), and let \(\pi\) be a probability mass function on \(\{0,1\}^2\). For \(M\) iid draws, the retained-rectangle probability is
\[
\begin{aligned}
Q_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)
={}&\sum_{r=0}^{k_0-1}\sum_{s=0}^{k_1-1}
\sum_{t=\max\{0,r+s-M\}}^{\min\{r,s\}}
\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Empty ranges contribute zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for \(j>0\). With
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r<k_0}\sum_{s<k_1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
one has \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). Shared factorial preprocessing costs \(O(M)\); a multinomial key costs \(O(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) time and \(O(M)\) memory, while the rolling alternative costs \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) time and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory.

For a finite key set \(\Pi\), first partition
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}},
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, respectively, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains exactly the remaining support-three keys admitted by the oriented zero-corner incompatibility prepass, and every key in the last two strata has support at least three and is not a face key. Vertices use the deterministic count formula. Edges use the exact binomial interval formula and charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\). Face keys use the exact charge \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator}\). Only the nonface support-at-least-three keys are assigned between the multinomial and rolling branches. Sequential processing has aggregate operation charge
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})
+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1
\right).
\]
The factorial table is built if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\), probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each multinomial key, and retention of one scalar per key uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi|+\max\{1,\ M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},\
k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\}
\right)
\]
memory. The vertex, edge, and oriented zero-corner face prepasses precede the downstream three-way dispatcher, so none of those strata enters a bivariate rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner coefficient branch. Face keys use only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace and do not trigger factorial preprocessing.

\[

\frac{M!}{(M-r-s+t)!(s-t)!(r-t)!t!}\\
&\qquad{}\times
\pi_{00}^{M-r-s+t}\pi_{01}^{s-t}\pi_{10}^{r-t}\pi_{11}^{t}.
\end{aligned}
\]
Empty ranges contribute zero, \(0^0=1\), and \(0^j=0\) for \(j>0\). With
\[
\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}
=\sum_{r<k_0}\sum_{s<k_1}
\left[\min\{r,s\}-\max\{0,r+s-M\}+1\right]_+,
\]
one has \(\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1}\le k_0k_1\min\{k_0,k_1\}\). Shared factorial preprocessing costs \(O(M)\); a multinomial key costs \(O(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})\) time and \(O(M)\) memory, while the rolling alternative costs \(O(Mk_0k_1)\) time and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory.

For a finite key set \(\Pi\), first partition
\[
\Pi=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}},
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, respectively, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains exactly the remaining support-three keys admitted by the oriented zero-corner incompatibility prepass, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains exactly the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and every key in the last two strata lies outside all four prepasses. Face keys use the exact charge \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\) from \(\texttt{lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator}\). Only keys outside all four prepasses are assigned between the multinomial and rolling branches. Sequential processing has aggregate operation charge
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}(M+\mathsf T_{M,k_0,k_1})
+|\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}|Mk_0k_1
\right).
\]
The factorial table is built if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\), probability-specific power tables are rebuilt for each multinomial key, and retention of one scalar per key uses
\[
O\!\left(
|\Pi|+\max\{1,\ M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\},\
k_0k_1\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{DP}}\ne\varnothing\}\}
\right)
\]
memory. The vertex, edge, oriented zero-corner face, and support-four rank-one prepasses precede the downstream three-way dispatcher, so none of those strata enters a bivariate rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner coefficient branch. Face and rank-one keys use only \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace and do not trigger factorial preprocessing.

changed.from:
Let \(\mathscr E_0\) and \(\mathscr E_1\) be finite sets, let \(R\) be a probability mass function on \(\mathscr E_0\times\mathscr E_1\), and let \(b_a:\mathscr E_a\to\{0,1\}\). Define \(\pi_{xy}=\sum_{e_0,e_1}R(e_0,e_1)\mathbf 1\{b_0(e_0)=x,b_1(e_1)=y\}\). For \(1\le k_0,k_1\le M+1\), if both ranks are at most \(M\), the retained recurrence
\[
d_0(0,0)=1,\qquad
d_{i+1}(r,s)=\sum_{x,y\in\{0,1\}}\pi_{xy}d_i(r-x,s-y)
\]
satisfies
\[
\sum_{r<k_0,s<k_1}d_M(r,s)
=\Pr\!\left\{\sum_i b_0(E_{i,0})<k_0,\ \sum_i b_1(E_{i,1})<k_1\right\}.
\]
After \(R\) is tabulated, this generic recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_0k_1)\) operations and \(O(k_0k_1)\) memory. With exactly one boundary rank, the other one-dimensional recurrence costs \(O(|\mathscr E_0||\mathscr E_1|+Mk_c)\) operations and \(O(k_c)\) memory; with two boundary ranks the event is certain.

For a finite collection \(\Pi\) of four-cell keys, first evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\}\) by
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}
\]
at its unique support point \((x,y)\). Next evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\notin\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}\) as follows. For a deterministically oriented support \(\{u,v\}\), put \(p=\pi_v\), \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), \(c_a=Mu_a\), and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\). Then the two terminal counts are
\[
R_M=c_0+d_0N,\qquad S_M=c_1+d_1N,
\]
and their strict inequalities are equivalent to the explicit interval \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\), with the stated empty-interval convention. Hence
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
The vertex branch costs \(O(1)\) exact time and workspace; the edge branch costs \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace. Deterministic orientation, evaluator selection, and tie-breaking preserve exactness. For every remaining support-three key, inspect the four corner orientations. If \(\pi^\sigma_{11}=0\) and \(t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\), then \(T_0^\sigma+T_1^\sigma\le M\), so the two transformed failure events are disjoint and
\[
J_\sigma=1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
-\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
The exact shorter binomial-tail recurrences and the existing corner identities evaluate the original lower--lower probability in \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace; reconstruction reuses complements of the two evaluated tails. Deterministic charge minimization and lexicographic tie-breaking preserve exactness. Only two-finite-rank support-at-least-three keys outside this face prepass can require a generic bivariate evaluator.

D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}
\]
at its unique support point \((x,y)\). Next evaluate \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\notin\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}\) as follows. For a deterministically oriented support \(\{u,v\}\), put \(p=\pi_v\), \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), \(c_a=Mu_a\), and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\). Then the two terminal counts are
\[
R_M=c_0+d_0N,\qquad S_M=c_1+d_1N,
\]
and their strict inequalities are equivalent to the explicit interval \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\), with the stated empty-interval convention. Hence
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
The vertex branch costs \(O(1)\) exact time and workspace; the edge branch costs \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace. Deterministic orientation, evaluator selection, and tie-breaking preserve exactness. For every remaining support-three key, inspect the four corner orientations. If \(\pi^\sigma_{11}=0\) and \(t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\), then \(T_0^\sigma+T_1^\sigma\le M\), so the two transformed failure events are disjoint and
\[
J_\sigma=1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
-\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
The exact shorter binomial-tail recurrences and the existing corner identities evaluate the original lower--lower probability in \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) workspace; reconstruction reuses complements of the two evaluated tails. Deterministic charge minimization and lexicographic tie-breaking preserve exactness. Only two-finite-rank support-at-least-three keys outside this face prepass can require a generic bivariate evaluator.

For every remaining support-four key satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), define \(\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11}\), \(\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11}\), \(\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\}\), and \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1\). Exact determinant testing and the factorization
\[
\pi_{00}+\pi_{10}x+\pi_{01}y+\pi_{11}xy
=\{(1-\theta_0)+\theta_0x\}\{(1-\theta_1)+\theta_1y\}
\]
give
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1)
\]
in \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace. Such a key is evaluated after the vertex, edge, and incompatible-face strata and before any rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch.

Thus only two-finite-rank keys outside the vertex, edge, incompatible-face, and rank-one strata can require a generic bivariate evaluator.
changed.reason: The residual bivariate dispatcher currently includes full-support determinant-zero keys even though exact factorization gives a distinct constant-workspace product-binomial prepass. The proposed claim narrows the residual dispatcher to keys outside that exact stratum and records the resulting valid resource improvement without changing the probability object.

changed.from:
Suppose \(k_0,k_1\le M\). Define the arm-orbit sets \(\mathcal O_a\), their sizes \(m_a\), the joint calibration-orbit law
\[
R_w(O_0,O_1)=\sum_z\nu(z)\kappa_{w,0}(O_0\mid z)\kappa_{w,1}(O_1\mid z),
\]
and the coherent common-target law
\[
P_T(O_0,O_1)=\sum_{v\in\mathcal V}q_{\mathrm{tar}}(v)
\mathbf 1\{O_0(v)=O_0,\ O_1(v)=O_1\}.
\]
Let \(L=|\operatorname{supp}(P_T)|\le\min\{|\mathcal V|,m_0m_1\}\). For every preorder pair \(C=(c_0,c_1)\), let \(P_T^C\), \(L_C\), the strict-prefix keys \(\Pi_C\), \(U_C\), and the local weights \(\omega_C\) be the corresponding pushforwards. Then \(U_C\le L_C\le\min\{L,d_0d_1\}\), and
\[
\Pr\{\text{both rank events under }C\}
=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi).
\]
A global memo is keyed exactly by \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\); the fourth cell is determined by normalization, and local weights never enter the key. Thus the unchanged \(R_w\), unchanged coherent \(P_T^C\), common selector, common target, and within-replicate cross-arm dependence are preserved.

Let \(\Pi_\star=\bigcup_C\Pi_C\), \(G=|\Pi_\star|\), and partition it first into the support-one stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), then the support-two stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), then the eligible support-three face stratum \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\), and finally the remaining support-at-least-three strata \(\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}},\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}},\Pi_+\) chosen by deterministic least-charge comparison. Put
\[
h_a=M-k_a+1,\qquad \bar k_a=\min\{k_a,h_a\},\qquad
\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}
\min\{\bar k_0,i+1\}\min\{\bar k_1,i+1\}.
\]
Vertices use the deterministic formula; edges use the exact binomial interval charge \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)\); eligible support-three face keys use \(\mathsf F_M(\pi)\); and only the remaining nonface support-at-least-three keys enter the rolling, zero-cell-safe multinomial, or positive-corner branches. With prespecified tie-breaking,
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
and
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
\left(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1}\right)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
If \(\sigma(\pi)\) minimizes the positive-corner charge, define
\[
\mathsf W=\max\!\left(
\{0\}\cup\{\bar k_0\bar k_1:\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{M:\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
\cup\{\min(t_0^{\sigma(\pi)},t_1^{\sigma(\pi)}):\pi\in\Pi_+\}
\right).
\]
Vertices, edges, and face keys contribute only \(O(1)\) workspace, and the factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\). The face stratum contributes no nonconstant term to \(\mathsf W\). With \(\mathfrak C\) the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs, exact enumeration after \(R_w\) is tabulated costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_{C\in\mathfrak C}[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
and its uniform version replaces the middle preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}].
\]
Working memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\).

There is a strict admissible edge improvement. Let \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
The edge evaluator uses \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the least-charge former nonvertex dispatcher uses \(\Theta(M)\) arithmetic. This key is realized by two audited pair blocks under \(S_2^2\), independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment in each block, a singleton selector library whose legal equivariant arm-\(1\) rule selects either block equiprobably and then its unique observed arm-\(1\) member, a target supported in block \(2\), an arm-\(0\) preorder with block \(2\) lowest, and an arm-\(1\) preorder with block \(1\) below block \(2\). Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is also a strict admissible zero-corner face improvement for every \(M\ge2\). Let \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The \(LL\) face orientation has \(\pi_{11}=0\) and \(t_0+t_1=2M>M\), so the face evaluator costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace. The rolling, multinomial, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation each cost \(\Theta(M)\). The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial finite group, a known law uniform on three assignment vectors having one treated member per block, deterministic legal observable selector lookups whose strict-prefix pairs are \(00\), \(01\), and \(10\), a singleton common selector library, and a fixed common target in block \(2\). In each arm, place both block-\(1\) singleton orbits strictly below the target orbit and tie the block-\(2\) singleton orbits with the target. Taking
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

\[

\]

\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
gives the stated ranks and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is also a strict admissible zero-corner face improvement for every \(M\ge2\). Let \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) in \(00,01,10,11\) order. Then
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The \(LL\) face orientation has \(\pi_{11}=0\) and \(t_0+t_1=2M>M\), so the face evaluator costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace. The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial finite group, a known law uniform on three assignment vectors having one treated member per block, deterministic legal observable selector lookups whose strict-prefix pairs are \(00\), \(01\), and \(10\), a singleton common selector library, and a fixed common target in block \(2\). \[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
gives \(k_0=k_1=M\) and satisfies the error-budget assumptions.

There is a strict admissible rank-one support-four improvement. Let \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\). Then
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The rank-one prepass uses \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch uses \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The key is realized by two pair blocks with the trivial group, independent fair one-treated--one-control assignments, a singleton selector choosing block \(1\)'s unique control in arm \(0\) and block \(2\)'s unique treated member in arm \(1\), the common target \(v_{2,0}\), and preorders making one block-\(1\) control singleton orbit and the block-\(2\) mate-treatment singleton orbit strictly below their target orbits. \[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}.
\]

The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps for ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. If both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, and the last three contain only remaining nonface support-at-least-three keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge or face term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face stratum neither triggers that table nor adds nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the frontier is \(1-\alpha\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the frontier is \(1-\alpha\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.

changed.from:
{"id":"def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","name":"simplex vertex-and-edge exact-key prepass","construction":"For a finite set \(\Pi_\star\) of probability mass functions on \(\{0,1\}^2\), first set
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=1\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}=\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}:|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=2\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-three key and every corner orientation \(\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2\), let \(\pi^\sigma\) be the transformed four-cell law and let \(t_a^\sigma=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a^\sigma=M-k_a+1\) under \(U\). Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[
J_\sigma=
1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace. Only keys in
\(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}})\)
enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.","inputs":["\(\Pi_\star\)","\(M\)","\(k_0\)","\(k_1\)"]}

\[

\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\), let \(u=(x,y)\) be its unique support point and return \(\mathbf 1\{Mx<k_0,\ My<k_1\}\). For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\), order its two support points \(u=(u_0,u_1)\) and \(v=(v_0,v_1)\) deterministically; both possible orientations may be compared, with the smaller certified charge chosen and lexicographic tie-breaking. Put \(p=\pi_v\in(0,1)\), let \(N\sim\operatorname{Bin}(M,p)\), and define \(c_a=Mu_a\) and \(d_a=v_a-u_a\) for \(a\in\{0,1\}\). Set
\[
L_\pi=\max\!\left(\{0\}\cup\{c_a-k_a+1:d_a=-1\}\right),\qquad
U_\pi=\min\!\left(\{M\}\cup\{k_a-c_a-1:d_a=1\}\right).
\]
If \(d_a=0\) and \(c_a\ge k_a\) for some \(a\), declare \([L_\pi,U_\pi]\) empty. Otherwise return
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=\Pr\{L_\pi\le N\le U_\pi\}.
\]
For a nonempty interval define
\[
\eta_\pi=\min\!\left\{
\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi\}+U_\pi-L_\pi+1,\ 
L_\pi+M-U_\pi,\ 
\min\{U_\pi+1,M-U_\pi\}+\min\{L_\pi,M-L_\pi+1\}
\right\},
\]
and set \(\eta_\pi=0\) for an empty interval. Direct interval summation, complementary left-and-right tails, and a difference of two shorter binomial tails are three exact alternatives; choose the least charge deterministically and write \(\mathsf E_M(\pi)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\eta_\pi\). The edge evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf E_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-three key and every corner orientation \(\sigma\in\{L,U\}^2\), let \(\pi^\sigma\) be the transformed four-cell law and let \(t_a^\sigma=k_a\) under \(L\) and \(t_a^\sigma=M-k_a+1\) under \(U\). Define
\[
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)=
\{\sigma:\pi^\sigma_{11}=0,\ t_0^\sigma+t_1^\sigma>M\},
\qquad
\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}):
\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\ne\varnothing\}.
\]
For \(\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)\), let \(T_a^\sigma\) be the transformed terminal count and set
\[
J_\sigma=
1-\Pr\{T_0^\sigma\ge t_0^\sigma\}
 -\Pr\{T_1^\sigma\ge t_1^\sigma\}.
\]
Reconstruct \(D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)\) from \(J_\sigma\) by the \(LL\), \(LU\), \(UL\), or \(UU\) corner identity. Each required reconstruction marginal is the complement of one of the two tails already evaluated. With
\[
\ell_M(t)=1+\lceil\log_2 M\rceil+\min\{t,M-t+1\},
\qquad
\mathsf F_M(\pi)=1+
\min_{\sigma\in\Sigma_{\mathrm{face}}(\pi)}
\{\ell_M(t_0^\sigma)+\ell_M(t_1^\sigma)\},
\]
choose a minimizing orientation and break ties lexicographically. The face evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf F_M(\pi))\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace.

For every remaining support-four key, test the exact memoized-cell identity
\[
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}.
\]
Define
\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}=
\{\pi\in\Pi_\star\setminus
(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}):
|\operatorname{supp}(\pi)|=4,\ 
\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\}.
\]
For \(\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\), put
\[
\theta_0=\pi_{10}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\theta_1=\pi_{01}+\pi_{11},\qquad
\bar k_a=\min\{k_a,M-k_a+1\},
\]
and evaluate
\[
D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi)=
H_{M,k_0}(\theta_0)H_{M,k_1}(\theta_1),
\qquad
H_{M,k}(\theta)=\Pr\{\operatorname{Bin}(M,\theta)<k\}.
\]
With
\[
\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+\bar k_0+\bar k_1,
\]
the rank-one evaluation uses \(O(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1})\) exact arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary workspace, with deterministic exact testing and no approximation. Only keys in
\(\Pi_\star\setminus(\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\cup\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}})\)
enter the remaining rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner dispatcher.","inputs":["\(\Pi_\star\)","\(M\)","\(k_0\)","\(k_1\)"]}

directive:
[SELECTOR KERNEL SUPPORT-DOMAIN REPAIR — PRESERVE ALL RESULTS]
1. Correct ass:observable-selector-kernel so normalization, nonnegativity, and realized-arm support are required for assignments z in supp(nu), equivalently nu(z)>0. Kernels may be defined only on supp(nu), or extended arbitrarily off support; off-support values must not enter q^{obs}_{h,a}.
2. Correct ass:selector-equivariance on the same invariant support: require the equality for z in supp(nu) (then gz is also in supp(nu) by ass:known-matched-randomization). Do not strengthen the assignment law or selector requirements.
3. Synchronize every definition/proof that writes q^{obs}_{h,a} or integrates kappa against nu so it explicitly ignores arbitrary off-support extensions where needed.
4. In the strict support-three face and support-four rank-one audits inside lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator and downstream frontier proofs, identify the displayed lookup rules as complete kernels on supp(nu).

changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps for ranks \(k_0,k_1\); no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. If both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the frontier is zero. Binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the frontier is \(1-\alpha\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.

changed.from:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst-coverage deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps, with no asserted equality to a supremum over all invariant latent laws. It is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains remaining support-four determinant-zero keys, and the last three strata contain only residual keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, face keys use the incompatibility evaluator, rank-one keys use the product-binomial evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k,k}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a residual key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); rank-one keys do not trigger it.

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the diagonal face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) at \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
It has the trivial-group two-pair realization audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). The face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas each former branch costs \(\Theta(M)\).

The admissible off-diagonal audit \(M=2n\), \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\) remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator: the rank-one branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) time and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the former least-charge branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) time and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The diagonal partition uses the same exact determinant test and the charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\).
changed.to:
The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst joint-armwise-inclusion certificate deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps, with no asserted equality to the actual effect-set coverage deficit or to a supremum over all invariant latent laws. For the symmetric ordinary rank construction,
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold, as shown by \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\). It is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains remaining support-four determinant-zero keys, and the last three strata contain only residual keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, face keys use the incompatibility evaluator, rank-one keys use the product-binomial evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k,k}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a residual key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); rank-one keys do not trigger it.

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the diagonal face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) at \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
It has the trivial-group two-pair realization audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). The face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas each former branch costs \(\Theta(M)\).

The admissible off-diagonal audit \(M=2n\), \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\) remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator: the rank-one branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) time and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the former least-charge branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) time and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The diagonal partition uses the same exact determinant test and the charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\).

The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst joint-armwise-inclusion certificate deficit over bounded orbit-constant score maps for ranks \(k_0,k_1\); it is not the actual effect-set coverage deficit, and no equality with a supremum over all \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. For the ordinary asymmetric rank construction, simultaneous armwise inclusion implies effect-set inclusion pointwise:
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold: with \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\), one has \(\tau_T=0\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) although both armwise inclusions fail. If both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. &+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}

+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),

\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. \[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)

+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}

\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[

the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels

\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the joint-armwise-inclusion certificate deficit is zero. For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies

D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad

For the ordinary asymmetric rank construction, simultaneous armwise inclusion implies effect-set inclusion pointwise:
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold: with \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\), one has \(\tau_T=0\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) although both armwise inclusions fail. This pointwise containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. Within the deterministic submodel, if both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

Within the deterministic submodel, if exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the deterministic-submodel deficit is zero. Within this submodel, binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the deterministic-submodel deficit is \(1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.
changed.reason: The frozen claim calls the deterministic orbit-score optimization a certificate without stating that arbitrary invariant laws may have stochastic conditional residual scores. The correction restricts exactness and every frontier value to the submodel actually optimized, preserves the event containment and evaluator, and separates the already-proved full-model guarantees.

note: Codex ACCEPT_SUBSET proposal-87: accepted the two deterministic-submodel theorem narrowings and paired proposed proofs; rejected status/metadata-regressing statement replacements and deferred reverse dependencies.

directive: Finish only the structural synchronization required by proposal-87. For each theorem, emit a metadata-only statement-replace that echoes the then-current OLD/current narrowed claim byte-for-byte, has status "proved" with its complete proof, and PRESERVES the current corrected/synchronized justification, gap, and consumer that deny Gamma full-model certificate semantics; do not reuse the rejected stale fields that call it an unrestricted joint-inclusion certificate or suggest transfer to effect coverage. Change only direct dependencies needed by the accepted proof: add def:invariant-laws, thm:common-selector-lp, and prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound where directly cited, retaining all existing valid dependencies. The asymmetric and symmetric proof text must continue to distinguish the deterministic orbit-constant-score Gamma evaluator from arbitrary stochastic within-orbit residual laws, retain pointwise event containment as a separate fact, and retain only Bonferroni/active-arm-TV guarantees for the full model. Rebuild metadata:reverse-dependencies last. Do not change any other theorem, definition, symbol, citation, computation, or prose claim.

with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

Within the deterministic submodel, if exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the deterministic-submodel deficit is zero. Within this submodel, binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the deterministic-submodel deficit is \(1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary","prop:asymmetric-disjoint-orbit-witness","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-equivariance","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator","lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","thm:common-selector-lp","prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound"],"status":"proved","justification":"The theorem exactly evaluates the worst joint-armwise-inclusion certificate deficit over coherent bounded orbit-constant scores for arbitrary prespecified arm budgets.","gap":"The certificate is a sufficient-event audit, not the actual effect-set coverage deficit; marginal or arm-independent computation would also lose common-target and within-replicate cross-arm coupling.","consumer":"Use the asymmetric preorder and memoized four-cell evaluator for a sharp finite audit of the joint-inclusion certificate, and transfer it to effect-set coverage only through the proved event containment.","proof_tex":"Put
\[
J(s_0,s_1)=\Pr\!\left\{s_1(O_{T,1})\le Q_{k_1}(s_1(O_{1,1}),\ldots,s_1(O_{M,1}))\ \text{and}\ s_0(O_{T,0})\le Q_{k_0}(s_0(O_{1,0}),\ldots,s_0(O_{M,0}))\right\}.
\]
By \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\),
\[
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)
=\max_{s_0,s_1}\bigl[(1-\alpha)-J(s_0,s_1)\bigr]_+,
\tag{1}
\]
where the maximum ranges only over deterministic maps \(s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\). Thus (1) is, by construction, the exact worst deficit of the displayed joint rank event in that deterministic orbit-score submodel.

That submodel is strictly narrower than the score behavior allowed by \(\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\). For example, fix the covariates, take both predictors to be zero, draw a nondegenerate Bernoulli variable \(\xi_i\), and set \(Y_{iv}(a)=K\xi_i\) for every member \(v\) and arm \(a\). This law is bounded and invariant under every member relabelling, but, conditional on any selected orbit, its residual score is the nondegenerate random variable \(K\xi_i\). Therefore an arbitrary law in the full model need not induce a deterministic map \(s_a\), and the optimization domain in (1) is not the full-model domain. Hence (1) supplies neither a uniform full-model joint-inclusion certificate nor a causal effect-set coverage certificate.

The event containment in the theorem is nevertheless valid for every realization. Define
\[
E_{\mathrm{joint}}=\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\},
\qquad
E_{\mathrm{eff}}=\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\}.
\]
On \(E_{\mathrm{joint}}\), the definition of the Minkowski difference and \(\tau_T=Y_{0T}(1)-Y_{0T}(0)\) give
\[
\tau_T\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}-I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}
=\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w.
\tag{2}
\]
Thus \(E_{\mathrm{joint}}\subseteq E_{\mathrm{eff}}\), and monotonicity of conditional probability proves the stated inequality. The reverse containment fails when both intervals are \(\{0\}\) and both potential outcomes equal \(K\): then both armwise inclusions fail, whereas \(\tau_T=0\) belongs to the difference set \(\{0\}\). Equation (2) is algebraic and does not enlarge the optimization domain in (1).

Suppose first that \(k_0,k_1\le M\). For a deterministic score map, the order-statistic identity
\[
s_a(O_{T,a})\le Q_{k_a}(s_a(O_{1,a}),\ldots,s_a(O_{M,a}))
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\sum_{i=1}^M\mathbf 1\{s_a(O_{i,a})<s_a(O_{T,a})\}<k_a
\tag{3}
\]
shows that the event depends on \(s_a\) only through its total preorder on the finite set \(\mathcal O_a\). Conversely, if the ordered nonempty tie classes are \(C_{a,1}\prec\cdots\prec C_{a,d_a}\), assign score zero when \(d_a=1\), and otherwise assign \((j-1)K/(d_a-1)\) to class \(C_{a,j}\). This realizes the preorder in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels. The maximum in (1) is therefore attained by one of the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) preorder pairs.

For each such pair, \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) proves
\[
J(C)=\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_C}\omega_C(\pi)D_{M,k_0,k_1}(\pi),
\tag{4}
\]
while retaining the full calibration law \(R_w\), the coherent common-target pushforward \(P_T^C\), and the local weights. That lemma, together with \(\texttt{lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program}\), \(\texttt{lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative}\), and \(\texttt{lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator}\), proves the seven disjoint key strata, every displayed exact evaluator, the branch charge \(\mathsf B\), both enumeration bounds, and memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). It also proves that face and rank-one keys use constant auxiliary workspace and do not trigger factorial preprocessing. The same coherent-evaluator lemma proves the edge, face, and rank-one audit identities, their legal two-block realizations, their displayed level choices, and each strict resource comparison.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), \(\texttt{prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary}\) makes the boundary-arm event certain. For \(c=1-b\), conditioning (3) on \(O_{T,c}=O_t\) gives a binomial count with success probability
\[
\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}.
\]
Averaging over the target orbit gives the formula in the statement. The ordered-class scan and exact shorter-tail recurrence in \(\texttt{lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator}\) give the asserted \(F_{m_c}\)-preorder time and constant auxiliary distribution-function memory. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), both rank events are certain and (1) equals zero.

For completeness, the claimed failure of binary attainment follows from a finite exact witness. Use four audited pair blocks with independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment, calibration block masses
\[
q=(13/20,1/20,1/4,1/20),
\]
and common-target block masses
\[
p=(1/20,1/10,1/4,3/5).
\]
Choose a block according to \(q\) and then its unique observed member in the requested arm. This is legal and equivariant on the invariant support. Take arm \(0\) constant and order arm \(1\) as \(1\prec3\prec2\prec4\). At \(M=3\) and \(k_0=k_1=3\), the joint-event failure probability is
\[
\frac14\left(\frac{13}{20}\right)^3
+\frac1{10}\left(\frac{18}{20}\right)^3
+\frac35\left(\frac{19}{20}\right)^3
=\frac{104957}{160000}.
\]
Since \(1-\alpha=1/2\), its four-level objective is \(24957/160000\). For binary high-score sets \(H_0,H_1\), inclusion--exclusion gives failure probability
\[
p(H_0)\{1-q(H_0)\}^3+p(H_1)\{1-q(H_1)\}^3
-p(H_0\cap H_1)\{1-q(H_0)\}^3\{1-q(H_1)\}^3.
\tag{5}
\]
Exact enumeration of the sixteen choices for each high set gives maximum objective \(759109/5000000\). Direct subtraction yields
\[
\frac{24957}{160000}-\frac{759109}{5000000}
=\frac{83189}{20000000}>0,
\]
so binary maps do not attain (1).

If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, assign score \(K\) to every target-supported orbit and zero to every calibration-supported orbit in that arm, and use zero in the other arm. The first rank event fails surely and the second holds surely. Hence \(J=0\), so (1) equals \(1-\alpha\), its largest possible value.

Finally, the full-model statements remain separate. Under exact orbit balance, \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) proves the Bonferroni coverage guarantee for the ordinary rank construction. Without exact balance, \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) proves the active-arm total-variation lower bound. Neither result invokes (1). Setting \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\) gives \(k_0=k_1=k\) by \(\texttt{lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase}\), which yields the symmetric deterministic-submodel specialization. Every computational prepass changes only the exact evaluation of (4), so it changes none of the separate active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics."}

This exactness is solely over deterministic maps \(s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\). For an arbitrary \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), a residual score can remain stochastic conditional on its orbit, so \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is not a uniform joint-inclusion certificate and is not a causal effect-set coverage certificate for the full model; no equality with a supremum over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. For the ordinary asymmetric rank construction, simultaneous armwise inclusion implies effect-set inclusion pointwise:
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold: with \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\), one has \(\tau_T=0\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) although both armwise inclusions fail. This pointwise containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. Within the deterministic submodel, if both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.
