\tag{4}
\]
while retaining the full calibration law \(R_w\), the coherent common-target pushforward \(P_T^C\), and the local weights. That lemma, together with \(\texttt{lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program}\), \(\texttt{lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative}\), and \(\texttt{lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator}\), proves the seven disjoint key strata, every displayed exact evaluator, the branch charge \(\mathsf B\), both enumeration bounds, and memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). It also proves that face and rank-one keys use constant auxiliary workspace and do not trigger factorial preprocessing. The same coherent-evaluator lemma proves the edge, face, and rank-one audit identities, their legal two-block realizations, their displayed level choices, and each strict resource comparison.

If exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), \(\texttt{prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary}\) makes the boundary-arm event certain. For \(c=1-b\), conditioning (3) on \(O_{T,c}=O_t\) gives a binomial count with success probability
\[
\theta(O_t)=\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}.
\]
Averaging over the target orbit gives the formula in the statement. The ordered-class scan and exact shorter-tail recurrence in \(\texttt{lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator}\) give the asserted \(F_{m_c}\)-preorder time and constant auxiliary distribution-function memory. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), both rank events are certain and (1) equals zero.

For completeness, the claimed failure of binary attainment follows from a finite exact witness. Use four audited pair blocks with independent fair one-treated--one-control assignment, calibration block masses
\[
q=(13/20,1/20,1/4,1/20),
\]
and common-target block masses
\[
p=(1/20,1/10,1/4,3/5).
\]
Choose a block according to \(q\) and then its unique observed member in the requested arm. This is legal and equivariant on the invariant support. Take arm \(0\) constant and order arm \(1\) as \(1\prec3\prec2\prec4\). At \(M=3\) and \(k_0=k_1=3\), the joint-event failure probability is
\[
\frac14\left(\frac{13}{20}\right)^3
+\frac1{10}\left(\frac{18}{20}\right)^3
+\frac35\left(\frac{19}{20}\right)^3
=\frac{104957}{160000}.
\]
Since \(1-\alpha=1/2\), its four-level objective is \(24957/160000\). For binary high-score sets \(H_0,H_1\), inclusion--exclusion gives failure probability
\[
p(H_0)\{1-q(H_0)\}^3+p(H_1)\{1-q(H_1)\}^3
-p(H_0\cap H_1)\{1-q(H_0)\}^3\{1-q(H_1)\}^3.
\tag{5}
\]
Exact enumeration of the sixteen choices for each high set gives maximum objective \(759109/5000000\). Direct subtraction yields
\[
\frac{24957}{160000}-\frac{759109}{5000000}
=\frac{83189}{20000000}>0,
\]
so binary maps do not attain (1).

If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, assign score \(K\) to every target-supported orbit and zero to every calibration-supported orbit in that arm, and use zero in the other arm. The first rank event fails surely and the second holds surely. Hence \(J=0\), so (1) equals \(1-\alpha\), its largest possible value.

Finally, the full-model statements remain separate. Under exact orbit balance, \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) proves the Bonferroni coverage guarantee for the ordinary rank construction. Without exact balance, \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) proves the active-arm total-variation lower bound. Neither result invokes (1). Setting \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\) gives \(k_0=k_1=k\) by \(\texttt{lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase}\), which yields the symmetric deterministic-submodel specialization. Every computational prepass changes only the exact evaluation of (4), so it changes none of the separate active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics."}
changed.reason: Synchronize the accepted narrowed deterministic-submodel theorem with its complete proof and add only the direct dependencies used to distinguish the full invariant model and its separate Bonferroni and active-arm total-variation guarantees.

{"id":"thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"Within the equal-split deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel optimized by \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\), the functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst deficit of the joint rank-inclusion event. This exactness is solely over deterministic maps. For an arbitrary \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), residual scores can remain stochastic conditional on their orbits, so \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is not a uniform joint-inclusion certificate and is not a causal effect-set coverage certificate for the full model; no equality with actual effect-set coverage or with a supremum over invariant latent laws is asserted. For the symmetric ordinary rank construction,
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold, as shown by \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\). This containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. The functional is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains remaining support-four determinant-zero keys, and the last three strata contain only residual keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, face keys use the incompatibility evaluator, rank-one keys use the product-binomial evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k,k}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a residual key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); rank-one keys do not trigger it.

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Within the deterministic submodel, binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Within the deterministic submodel, under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the diagonal face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) at \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
It has the trivial-group two-pair realization audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). The face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas each former branch costs \(\Theta(M)\).

The admissible off-diagonal audit \(M=2n\), \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\) remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator: the rank-one branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) time and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the former least-charge branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) time and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The diagonal partition uses the same exact determinant test and the charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\).","depends_on":["thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-equivariance","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","prop:disjoint-orbit-witness","prop:rank-boundary","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","thm:common-selector-lp","prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound"],"status":"proved","justification":"This is the equal-split specialization of the asymmetric joint-armwise-inclusion certificate frontier with the same coherent common-target semantics.","gap":"Binary scores need not attain the certificate frontier, and boundary ranks require their separate constant-event treatment.","consumer":"Use the symmetric evaluator only on the equal-split diagonal; otherwise retain the asymmetric ranks and certificate interpretation.","proof_tex":"Set \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\). By \(\texttt{lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase}\),
\[
k_0=k_1=k,
\qquad
\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w).
\tag{1}
\]
The corrected scope, exact deterministic-preorder interpretation, common-target coherence, binary counterexample, and disjoint-support value therefore follow from \(\texttt{thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). In particular, the example with common nondegenerate bounded outcomes shows that an arbitrary invariant law can have stochastic residual scores conditional on an orbit; consequently the diagonal functional in (1) is not a uniform full-model joint-inclusion or effect-coverage certificate.

The pointwise event containment specializes to
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\}
=\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\}.
\tag{2}
\]
Conditional probability is monotone, proving the stated inequality. When both intervals are \(\{0\}\) and both potential outcomes equal \(K\), the right event in (2) holds and the left event fails, so equality need not hold. As in the asymmetric theorem, (2) is algebraic and does not extend the deterministic optimization in (1) to stochastic conditional score laws.

Assume \(k\le M\). Specializing the asymmetric seven-way partition gives the displayed disjoint strata. Since
\[
\bar k_0=\bar k_1=\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\},
\]
the rolling charge is \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2\). Substitution in the asymmetric branch sum gives exactly \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\), including the face and rank-one terms. The same substitution gives \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\); the vertex, edge, face, and rank-one evaluators use constant workspace, and the rank-one evaluator does not trigger factorial preprocessing. Substitution in the adaptive, uniform, and memory bounds of \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) proves all displayed diagonal resource bounds.

For every \(M\ge2\), taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) gives \(k=M\). The audited edge construction in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) realizes \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\), and hence
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}.
\]
Its edge charge is \(\Theta(\log M)\), while the former dispatcher charge is \(\Theta(M)\). At the same diagonal rank, the audited face construction realizes \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) and gives
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M},
\]
again with \(\Theta(\log M)\) time and constant workspace versus \(\Theta(M)\) for every former branch. The off-diagonal rank-one audit remains a strict certificate of the common evaluator's computational improvement; on the diagonal, the same determinant test has charge
\[
\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k.
\]
If \(k=M+1\), \(\texttt{prop:rank-boundary}\) makes both deterministic rank events certain, and thus \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

Within the deterministic submodel, the exact four-level and binary values and their positive gap, as well as the value \(1-\alpha\) under disjoint support, follow through (1) from the asymmetric theorem. For the full stochastic model, \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) supplies the exact-balance Bonferroni guarantee, and \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) supplies the active-arm total-variation bound. Neither follows from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\). Because every computational branch evaluates the same strict-prefix probability within the deterministic submodel, the common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, and weighted-orbit semantics remain unchanged."}

(B) Add explicit theorem-level, no-priority comparator paragraphs and synchronized justification/gap/consumer fields. For thm:orbit-balance-iff: contrast HemerikGoeman2018 and RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023 (finite permutation/group testing) and DobribanYu2025 (predictive inference under group symmetry) with this manuscript's iid replicated invariant latent arrays, assignment-based calibration selection, equality of calibration/deployment score laws, and universal orbit-mass iff with an orbit-separating necessity witness. (C) Add three primary comparator records and use them in the asymmetric-frontier comparison, with faithful source restatements and source provenance so D0.5 can audit them even without raw network access: 1. Regis Lebrun (2013), "Efficient time/space algorithm to compute rectangular probabilities of multinomial, multivariate hypergeometric and multivariate Polya distributions," Statistics and Computing 23(5):615-623, DOI 10.1007/s11222-012-9334-8. Faithful source restatement: it computes arbitrary rectangular probabilities for these multivariate discrete count laws with a time/space algorithm and compares accuracy, cost, and storage against prior exact methods. Timans, Straehle, Sakmann, Naesseth, and Nalisnick (2025), "Max-Rank: Efficient Multiple Testing for Conformal Prediction," AISTATS, PMLR 258:3898-3906. URL https://proceedings.mlr.press/v258/timans25a.html. 3. Schlembach, Smirnov, and Winands (2025), "Dynamic Conformal Prediction for Multi-Target Regression: Optimising Informational Efficiency under Joint Validity," PMLR 266:193-213. This manuscript instead exactly evaluates its explicitly restricted deterministic bounded orbit-constant joint rank-deficit functional with asymmetric ranks, coherent common target, and within-replicate cross-arm dependence. changed.from:
{"id":"thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"Within the equal-split deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel optimized by \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\), the functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst deficit of the joint rank-inclusion event. This exactness is solely over deterministic maps. For an arbitrary \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), residual scores can remain stochastic conditional on their orbits, so \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is not a uniform joint-inclusion certificate and is not a causal effect-set coverage certificate for the full model; no equality with actual effect-set coverage or with a supremum over invariant latent laws is asserted. For the symmetric ordinary rank construction,
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold, as shown by \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\). This containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. The functional is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains remaining support-four determinant-zero keys, and the last three strata contain only residual keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, face keys use the incompatibility evaluator, rank-one keys use the product-binomial evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k,k}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a residual key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); rank-one keys do not trigger it.

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Within the deterministic submodel, binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Within the deterministic submodel, under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the diagonal face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) at \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
It has the trivial-group two-pair realization audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). The face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas each former branch costs \(\Theta(M)\).

The admissible off-diagonal audit \(M=2n\), \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\) remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator: the rank-one branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) time and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the former least-charge branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) time and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The diagonal partition uses the same exact determinant test and the charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\).","depends_on":["thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-equivariance","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","prop:disjoint-orbit-witness","prop:rank-boundary","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","def:invariant-laws","thm:common-selector-lp","prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound"],"status":"proved","justification":"This is the equal-split specialization of the deterministic asymmetric joint rank-inclusion frontier, with the same coherent common-target semantics and no full-model certificate interpretation.","gap":"The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) does not cover stochastic conditional residual scores and is not a uniform full-model joint-inclusion or causal effect-set coverage certificate; within its deterministic submodel, binary scores need not attain the frontier and boundary ranks require separate treatment.","consumer":"Use the symmetric evaluator only on the equal-split deterministic orbit-score submodel. For full-model effect coverage, use \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) under exact balance or \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) under imbalance.","proof_tex":"Set \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\). By \(\texttt{lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase}\),
\[
k_0=k_1=k,\qquad\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w).
\tag{1}
\]
The deterministic-preorder interpretation, coherent common-target calculation, full-model disclaimer, binary counterexample, disjoint-support value, and comparator scope therefore specialize from \(\texttt{thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). In particular, the bounded common-Bernoulli construction there proves that the diagonal functional is not a uniform full-model joint-inclusion or causal effect-set coverage certificate.

The pointwise implication specializes to
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}\subseteq\{\tau_T\in I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\}=\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\}.
\tag{2}
\]
If both intervals are \(\{0\}\) and both outcomes equal \(K\), the right event holds while the left event fails.

When \(k\le M\), substitute \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) into the asymmetric seven-way partition. The rolling charge becomes \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2\), the branch sum becomes exactly \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\), and the maximum live workspace becomes \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\). The adaptive, uniform, and memory bounds follow from \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Vertex, edge, face, and rank-one keys use constant auxiliary workspace, and rank-one keys do not trigger factorial preprocessing.

For every \(M\ge2\), \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) gives \(k=M\). The audited edge key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) has
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M},
\]
with \(\Theta(\log M)\) time rather than \(\Theta(M)\). The audited face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) has
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M},
\]
with the same strict resource improvement. The off-diagonal rank-one audit remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator, while the diagonal charge is \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\). If \(k=M+1\), both rank events are certain and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

Equation (1) also transfers the exact four-level and binary values and their positive gap, as well as the value \(1-\alpha\) under disjoint support. The full-model guarantees remain those in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\). The literature comparison likewise specializes only as a comparison of different optimized objects. Therefore no branch change affects the common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics."}

{"id":"thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"Within the equal-split deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel optimized by \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\), the functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is the exact finite worst deficit of the joint rank-inclusion event. This exactness is solely over deterministic maps. For an arbitrary \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), residual scores can remain stochastic conditional on their orbits, so \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) is not a uniform joint-inclusion certificate and is not a causal effect-set coverage certificate for the full model; no equality with actual effect-set coverage or with a supremum over invariant latent laws is asserted. For the symmetric ordinary rank construction,
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold, as shown by \(I_{0,w}=I_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\). This containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. The functional is the equal-split diagonal of \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). If \(k\le M\), its maximum is attained by the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, with at most \(m_a\) score levels in arm \(a\), while retaining the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local weights, and exact memo keys.

Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains remaining support-four determinant-zero keys, and the last three strata contain only residual keys. Put \(\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) and
\[
\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2.
\]
Vertices use deterministic counts, edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator, face keys use the incompatibility evaluator, rank-one keys use the product-binomial evaluator, and only the remaining keys enter the three-way dispatcher. Define
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k,k}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k,k}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k,\bar k})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi).
\end{aligned}
\]
Let \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k^2\) when rolling is used, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is used, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. Vertices, edges, face keys, and rank-one keys add no nonconstant workspace. Exact enumeration costs
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}
\right),
\]
its uniform version replaces the preorder-specific terms by
\[
F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}],
\]
and memory is \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}})\). Shared factorial preprocessing occurs if and only if a residual key enters \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); rank-one keys do not trigger it.

For every \(M\ge2\), a diagonal strict edge witness is obtained from the audited two-pair realization by taking \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), and the same key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\): then \(D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M}\), the edge branch costs \(\Theta(\log M)\), and the former dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The asymmetric audit witness \(M=2n\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), together with its exact levels and legality checks, remains the strict off-diagonal certificate for the common evaluator. If \(k=M+1\), evaluation is constant and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\). Within the deterministic submodel, binary attainment is false with the exact four-level and binary values \(24957/160000\) and \(759109/5000000\) at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\). Within the deterministic submodel, under disjoint target and calibration orbit support in one arm and \(k\le M\), \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\). The prepasses change no common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the diagonal face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) at \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\), hence \(k=M\), satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
It has the trivial-group two-pair realization audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). The face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas each former branch costs \(\Theta(M)\).

The admissible off-diagonal audit \(M=2n\), \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\) remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator: the rank-one branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) time and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas the former least-charge branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) time and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The diagonal partition uses the same exact determinant test and the charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\).","depends_on":["thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","prop:disjoint-orbit-witness","prop:rank-boundary","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","def:invariant-laws","thm:common-selector-lp","prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound"],"status":"proved","justification":"This is the equal-split specialization of the deterministic asymmetric joint rank-inclusion frontier, with the same coherent common-target semantics and no full-model certificate interpretation.","gap":"The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)\) does not cover stochastic conditional residual scores and is not a uniform full-model joint-inclusion or causal effect-set coverage certificate; within its deterministic submodel, binary scores need not attain the frontier and boundary ranks require separate treatment.","consumer":"Use the symmetric evaluator only on the equal-split deterministic orbit-score submodel. For full-model effect coverage, use \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) under exact balance or \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) under imbalance.","proof_tex":"Set \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\alpha/2\). By \(\texttt{lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase}\),
\[
k_0=k_1=k,\qquad\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)=\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w).
\tag{1}
\]
The deterministic-preorder interpretation, coherent common-target calculation, full-model disclaimer, binary counterexample, disjoint-support value, and comparator scope therefore specialize from \(\texttt{thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). In particular, the bounded common-Bernoulli construction there proves that the diagonal functional is not a uniform full-model joint-inclusion or causal effect-set coverage certificate.

The pointwise implication specializes to
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I_{1,w},Y_{0T}(0)\in I_{0,w}\}\subseteq\{\tau_T\in I_{1,w}-I_{0,w}\}=\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C_w\}.
\tag{2}
\]
If both intervals are \(\{0\}\) and both outcomes equal \(K\), the right event holds while the left event fails.

When \(k\le M\), substitute \(\bar k_0=\bar k_1=\bar k=\min\{k,M-k+1\}\) into the asymmetric seven-way partition. The rolling charge becomes \(\mathsf C_{M,k,k}=\sum_{i=0}^{M-1}\min\{\bar k,i+1\}^2\), the branch sum becomes exactly \(\mathsf B_{\mathrm{sym}}\), and the maximum live workspace becomes \(\mathsf W_{\mathrm{sym}}\). The adaptive, uniform, and memory bounds follow from \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\). Vertex, edge, face, and rank-one keys use constant auxiliary workspace, and rank-one keys do not trigger factorial preprocessing.

For every \(M\ge2\), \(\alpha=2/(M+1)\) gives \(k=M\). The audited edge key \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\) has
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M},
\]
with \(\Theta(\log M)\) time rather than \(\Theta(M)\). The audited face key \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) has
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M},
\]
with the same strict resource improvement. The off-diagonal rank-one audit remains a strict certificate for the common evaluator, while the diagonal charge is \(\mathsf R_{M,k,k}=1+2\lceil\log_2M\rceil+2\bar k\). If \(k=M+1\), both rank events are certain and \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha}(w)=0\).

Equation (1) also transfers the exact four-level and binary values and their positive gap, as well as the value \(1-\alpha\) under disjoint support. The full-model guarantees remain those in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\). The literature comparison likewise specializes only as a comparison of different optimized objects. Therefore no branch change affects the common-target, active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics."}

directive:
Complete the final coherent repair, with no mathematical claim change and no priority claim. The Gao cited-leaf source repair is now canonical and MUST precede/serve the asymmetric theorem.

1. Reissue thm:orbit-balance-iff and thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier with kind and statement byte-for-byte equal to the frozen proto and status exactly "to-prove" as required by d0_apply_change. Pair each with a complete proof that argues the proposed node. The asymmetric depends_on must consume all four comparator leaves, including canonical lem:structured-volume-dynamic-program-comparator.
2. Selector equivariance is not load-bearing. Remove ass:selector-equivariance from the core using the supported assumption-delete path only after all dependencies and literal reliance have been removed. Rewrite the orbit-balance proof's final support paragraph to say only: invariance of nu keeps positive-mass assignment support invariant; assignments outside support have zero integrated weight; arbitrary total kernel extensions there do not affect the mixtures; the proof is armwise. Do not say selector equivariance is used. In lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator and the asymmetric theorem's proof/audit examples, replace descriptions such as "legal equivariant rule", "equivariant on invariant support", and "equivariance is automatic" with the actually used facts: normalized lookup/kernel and requested realized-arm support on every positive-mass assignment; off-support extensions have zero integrated weight. In limitations and every other prose field, remove the claim that the theory retains or requires equivariant selector kernels. Preserve the examples and all probabilities unchanged.
3. Correct the two bibliography records exactly to the official PMLR author metadata verified on 2026-07-22: TimansEtAl2025 authors are Alexander Timans, Christoph-Nikolas Straehle, Kaspar Sakmann, Christian A. Naesseth, and Eric Nalisnick (not Christoph Straehle / Johannes Sakmann). SchlembachSmirnovWinands2025 authors are Filip Schlembach, Evgueni Smirnov, and Mark H. M. Winands (not Evgenii). Preserve titles, proceedings, volume, pages, year, and URLs.
4. Preserve the already correct weighted proof phrase "the likelihood ratio in (2)". Preserve deterministic-only frontier scope and theorem-level non-priority comparisons. Synchronize tldr, project_justification gap/niche/fill, related_work, honest_scope, and writeup. Rebuild reverse dependencies LAST after the assumption deletion and dependency rewrites. Emit no unrelated changes.

changed.from:
{"id":"thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"Within the deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel optimized by \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\), the functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst deficit of the joint rank-inclusion event for ranks \(k_0,k_1\). This exactness is solely over deterministic maps \(s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\). For an arbitrary \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), a residual score can remain stochastic conditional on its orbit, so \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is not a uniform joint-inclusion certificate and is not a causal effect-set coverage certificate for the full model; no equality with a supremum over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. For the ordinary asymmetric rank construction, simultaneous armwise inclusion implies effect-set inclusion pointwise:
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold: with \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\), one has \(\tau_T=0\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) although both armwise inclusions fail. This pointwise containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. Within the deterministic submodel, if both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

Within the deterministic submodel, if exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the deterministic-submodel deficit is zero. Within this submodel, binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the deterministic-submodel deficit is \(1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary","prop:asymmetric-disjoint-orbit-witness","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-equivariance","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator","lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","def:invariant-laws","thm:common-selector-lp","prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound"],"status":"to-prove","justification":"The theorem exactly evaluates the worst joint rank-inclusion deficit only within the deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel, for arbitrary prespecified arm budgets.","gap":"The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) does not cover stochastic conditional residual scores and is neither a uniform full-model joint-inclusion certificate nor a causal effect-set coverage certificate; marginal or arm-independent evaluation would also lose common-target and within-replicate cross-arm coupling.","consumer":"Use the asymmetric preorder and memoized four-cell evaluator only for the deterministic orbit-score frontier. For full-model effect coverage, use \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) under exact balance or \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) under imbalance; the event containment remains a separate pointwise implication."}
changed.to:
{"id":"thm:asymmetric-sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier","kind":"theorem","statement":"Within the deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel optimized by \(\texttt{def:coverage-frontier}\), the functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is the exact finite worst deficit of the joint rank-inclusion event for ranks \(k_0,k_1\). This exactness is solely over deterministic maps \(s_a:\mathcal O\to[0,K]\). For an arbitrary \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\), a residual score can remain stochastic conditional on its orbit, so \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) is not a uniform joint-inclusion certificate and is not a causal effect-set coverage certificate for the full model; no equality with a supremum over \(P\in\mathcal P_{\mathcal G,K}\) is asserted. For the ordinary asymmetric rank construction, simultaneous armwise inclusion implies effect-set inclusion pointwise:
\[
\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\}
\subseteq
\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\},
\qquad
\Pr_P\{\tau_T\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w\mid\mathcal T\}
\ge
\Pr_P\{Y_{0T}(1)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w},\ Y_{0T}(0)\in I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}\mid\mathcal T\}.
\]
Equality need not hold: with \(I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{0,w}=I^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_{1,w}=\{0\}\) and \(Y_{0T}(0)=Y_{0T}(1)=K\), one has \(\tau_T=0\in\mathcal C^{\boldsymbol\varepsilon}_w=\{0\}\) although both armwise inclusions fail. This pointwise containment is a separate algebraic fact and does not make \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) certify its premise for arbitrary invariant laws. Within the deterministic submodel, if both ranks are finite, the maximum is attained by enumerating the \(F_{m_0}F_{m_1}\) total-preorder pairs, every preorder has a representative in \([0,K]\) with at most \(m_a\) levels, and the coherent evaluator in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\) applies.

In that evaluator, retain the unchanged \(R_w\), coherent \(P_T^C\), local \(\omega_C\), exact-key sets \(\Pi_C\), union \(\Pi_\star\), and memo key \((M,k_0,k_1,\pi_{00},\pi_{01},\pi_{10})\). Partition
\[
\Pi_\star=\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}
\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}
\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\mathbin{\dot\cup}\Pi_+,
\]
where the first two strata have support sizes one and two, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}\) contains eligible support-three zero-corner keys, \(\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}\) contains the remaining support-four keys satisfying \(\pi_{00}\pi_{11}=\pi_{01}\pi_{10}\), and the last three contain only residual keys. Vertices use their deterministic counts; edges use the exact affine-binomial interval evaluator; face keys use the incompatibility evaluator in \(\texttt{def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass}\); rank-one keys use the product of two exact strict binomial CDFs with charge \(\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}\); the remaining keys use the prespecified least-charge rolling, multinomial-sum, or positive-corner branch. Put
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathsf B={}&|\Pi_{\mathrm{vert}}|
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{edge}}}\mathsf E_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{face}}}\mathsf F_M(\pi)
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{rank1}}}\mathsf R_{M,k_0,k_1}
+M\mathbf 1\{\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\ne\varnothing\}
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{roll}}}\mathsf C_{M,k_0,k_1}\\
&+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}}
(M+\mathsf T_{M,\bar k_0,\bar k_1})
+\sum_{\pi\in\Pi_+}\mathsf C_+(\pi),
\end{aligned}
\]
and let \(\mathsf W\) be the maximum of \(0\), \(\bar k_0\bar k_1\) when the rolling branch is nonempty, \(M\) when the multinomial branch is nonempty, and the selected positive-corner rolling widths. There is no additional edge, face, or rank-one term in \(\mathsf W\). The exact enumeration and uniform time bounds are
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}(m_0m_1+L)
+\sum_C[L_C\log(1+L_C)+L_C+U_C]
+\Bigl(\sum_CU_C\Bigr)\log(1+G)+\mathsf B
\right)
\]
and
\[
O\!\left(
|\mathcal V|+F_{m_0}F_{m_1}
[m_0m_1+L+L_{\max}\log(1+L_{\max})+L_{\max}
+U_{\max}\{1+\log(1+G)\}]
+\mathsf B
\right),
\]
with memory \(O(m_0m_1+L+U_{\max}+G+\mathsf W)\). The factorial table is built only when a remaining nonface support-at-least-three key is assigned to \(\Pi_{\mathrm{MS}}\); the face and rank-one strata neither trigger that table nor add nonconstant workspace.

For \(M=2n\ge4\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=M\), and \(\pi=(1/2,1/2,0,0)\),
\[
D_{M,n,M}(\pi)=1-2^{-M};
\]
the edge prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the former least-charge nonvertex dispatcher costs \(\Theta(M)\). The audited two-pair realization, selector, target, preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\quad
\varepsilon_1=\frac1{2n+1},\quad
\alpha=\frac{n+2}{2n+1}
\]
are those stated in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

Within the deterministic submodel, if exactly one rank \(k_b=M+1\), the boundary-arm event is certain and, for \(c=1-b\), the exact rank-event probability is
\[
\sum_{O_t\in\mathcal O_c}p_{c,O_t}
\Pr\!\left\{\operatorname{Bin}\!\left(M,
\sum_{O:s_c(O)<s_c(O_t)}q_{c,O}\right)<k_c\right\}.
\]
Enumeration uses only the \(F_{m_c}\) nonboundary preorders and costs
\[
O\!\left(F_{m_c}m_c[1+\log M+\min\{k_c,M-k_c+1\}]\right)
\]
arithmetic operations and \(O(1)\) auxiliary CDF memory after the two marginal vectors are tabulated. If both ranks equal \(M+1\), the deterministic-submodel deficit is zero. Within this submodel, binary attainment remains false: at \(M=3\), \(\alpha=1/2\), and \(\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=1/4\), a four-level objective is \(24957/160000\), every binary pair is at most \(759109/5000000\), and the gap is \(83189/20000000\). If target and calibration orbit supports are disjoint in a finite-rank arm, the deterministic-submodel deficit is \(1-\alpha\). For the full model, the valid guarantees are instead the exact-balance Bonferroni result in \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) and the active-arm total-variation bound in \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\); neither guarantee is derived from \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\). On the equal-split diagonal this theorem yields \(\texttt{thm:sharp-orbit-coverage-frontier}\). The computational prepasses alter no active-arm, pair-audit, empty-library, ordinary-rank, or weighted-orbit semantics.

For every \(M\ge2\), the admissible face key \(k_0=k_1=M\) and \(\pi=(1/3,1/3,1/3,0)\) satisfies
\[
D_{M,M,M}(\pi)=1-2\cdot3^{-M}.
\]
The zero-corner face prepass costs \(\Theta(\log M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, whereas rolling, multinomial summation, and every allowed positive-base coefficient orientation cost \(\Theta(M)\). The trivial-group two-pair assignment support, deterministic legal selector lookup, fixed block-\(2\) target, singleton-orbit preorders, and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\varepsilon_1=\frac1{M+1},
\qquad
\alpha=\frac2{M+1}
\]
are audited in \(\texttt{lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator}\).

For \(M=2n\) with \(n\ge3\), \(k_0=n\), \(k_1=n+2\), and \(\pi=(1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4)\), the exact determinant test selects the rank-one prepass and
\[
D_{2n,n,n+2}(\pi)=H_{2n,n}(1/2)H_{2n,n+2}(1/2).
\]
The new branch costs \(\Theta(M)\) exact arithmetic and \(O(1)\) workspace, while the least-charge former branch costs \(\Theta(M^2)\) arithmetic and \(\Theta(M)\) workspace. The legal two-block construction and levels
\[
\varepsilon_0=\frac{n+1}{2n+1},\qquad
\varepsilon_1=\frac{n-1}{2n+1},\qquad
\alpha=\frac{2n}{2n+1}
\]
are audited in the coherent evaluator.","depends_on":["lem:four-cell-rank-event-dynamic-program","lem:armwise-finite-rank-staircase","prop:asymmetric-rank-boundary","prop:asymmetric-disjoint-orbit-witness","ass:known-matched-randomization","ass:prespecified-target","ass:observable-selector-kernel","ass:selector-auxiliary-randomization","def:pair-action","def:observable-selector-mixture","def:coverage-frontier","def:finite-rank-error","def:rank-inversion","lem:one-boundary-orbit-frontier-evaluator","lem:exact-binomial-shorter-tail-evaluator","lem:coherent-two-finite-rank-frontier-evaluator","lem:four-cell-multinomial-sum-alternative","lem:corner-oriented-four-cell-rank-evaluator","def:simplex-vertex-key-prepass","def:invariant-laws","thm:common-selector-lp","prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound","lem:structured-volume-dynamic-program-comparator","lem:lebrun-rectangular-probability-comparator","lem:timans-max-rank-comparator","lem:schlembach-multi-target-allocation-comparator"],"status":"to-prove","justification":"The theorem exactly evaluates the worst joint rank-inclusion deficit only within the deterministic bounded orbit-constant-score submodel, for arbitrary prespecified arm budgets.","gap":"The functional \(\Gamma_{M,\alpha,\boldsymbol\varepsilon}(w)\) does not cover stochastic conditional residual scores and is neither a uniform full-model joint-inclusion certificate nor a causal effect-set coverage certificate; marginal or arm-independent evaluation would also lose common-target and within-replicate cross-arm coupling.","consumer":"Use the asymmetric preorder and memoized four-cell evaluator only for the deterministic orbit-score frontier. For full-model effect coverage, use \(\texttt{thm:common-selector-lp}\) under exact balance or \(\texttt{prop:active-arm-finite-rank-orbit-gap-bound}\) under imbalance; the event containment remains a separate pointwise implication."}
